item.title: CausalTimePrior：生成时间序列因果模型的合成数据框架
item.content:encoded:
提出CausalTimePrior生成时间序列因果数据，支持因果推断模型训练。



摘要： 本文提出CausalTimePrior，一个用于生成具有观测和干预时间序列的合成时间序列因果模型（TSCMs）的框架。该框架支持可配置的因果图结构、非线性自回归机制、状态切换动态以及多种干预类型。通过在CausalTimePrior上训练的Prior-data fitted networks（PFNs），能够对未见的TSCMs进行上下文因果效应估计，为时间序列因果推断的基模模型提供了可行路径。

item.title: ComVo：基于复数运算的神经声码器提升语音合成质量
item.content:encoded:
ComVo采用复数运算提升语音合成质量，训练效率提高25%。



摘要： ComVo是一种基于复数运算的神经声码器，通过使用原生复数算术的生成器和判别器，实现了结构化的对抗训练框架。该方法引入相位量化以规范相位变换，并提出块矩阵计算方案以减少冗余操作，从而提升训练效率。实验表明，ComVo在语音合成质量上优于现有实数基基线模型，且训练时间减少了25%。相关音频样本和代码已公开。

item.title: Paced框架提升知识蒸馏效率
item.content:encoded:
Paced通过优化蒸馏过程提升模型训练效率。



摘要： 文章指出传统知识蒸馏在计算资源上存在浪费，主要体现在学生模型已掌握的问题和超出其能力范围的问题上。作者提出Paced框架，通过调整蒸馏的pass-rate权重，将注意力集中在学生模型的最近发展区，从而提高蒸馏效率。理论分析表明，该方法具有最小最大鲁棒性，并在多个任务中表现出色，包括正向KL和反向KL蒸馏，以及两阶段协同策略。所有配置仅需学生模型的输出即可估计pass率，无需架构修改。

item.title: FrameVGGT：基于帧驱动的有限内存3D感知框架
item.content:encoded:
FrameVGGT在有限内存下实现稳定3D感知，提升长序列任务性能。



摘要： 本文提出FrameVGGT，一种基于帧驱动的滚动显式记忆框架，用于解决流式3D感知中KV缓存无限制增长的问题。通过将每帧的增量KV贡献视为连贯证据块，并将其压缩为紧凑原型，FrameVGGT在固定容量下维护互补帧块，同时可选轻量锚点处理罕见长时间退化情况。实验表明，在长序列3D重建、视频深度估计和相机位姿任务中，FrameVGGT在有限内存下实现了良好的准确率与内存权衡，并保持更稳定的几何结构。

item.title: 基于物理的强化学习与仿真训练在外骨骼控制中的应用验证
item.content:encoded:
研究提出RL框架和验证流程，验证仿真训练外骨骼控制器的有效性。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于物理的强化学习框架，用于学习减少生物关节力矩的外骨骼辅助策略，并开发了一个验证流程，利用公开步态数据集对训练后的控制网络进行推理和比较。研究中使用多层感知机控制器，针对平地和坡道行走，将双侧髋膝关节运动历史映射为归一化辅助扭矩。结果显示，预测的辅助扭矩在不同速度和坡度下保持任务强度趋势，尤其在髋关节表现出高度一致性。然而，在更高速度和更陡坡度下，差异增大，尤其是在膝关节处更为明显。延迟调优会影响正向功率注入，适度的时间偏移可提高特定步态阶段的一致性。该研究建立了仿真训练外骨骼控制器的定量验证框架，展示了良好的仿真到数据一致性，并指出了仿真到现实转移的潜力与挑战。

item.title: 基于残差动作的世界模型提升强化学习性能
item.content:encoded:
ResWM通过残差动作提升RL控制稳定性与效率。



摘要： 本文提出Residual-Action World Model（ResWM），将控制变量从绝对动作改为残差动作，即相对于上一步的增量调整。这种设计更符合现实世界控制的平滑性，减少搜索空间并提高长期规划的稳定性。同时引入Observation Difference Encoder，显式建模相邻帧的变化，生成与残差动作耦合的紧凑潜在动态。ResWM在Dreamer框架中实现，无需额外超参数。实验表明，ResWM在样本效率、最终回报和控制平滑性方面均优于Dreamer和TD-MPC，且生成的轨迹更稳定、能耗更低，对实际机器人部署具有重要意义。

item.title: PolyFormer：基于物理知识的优化模型新方法
item.content:encoded:
PolyFormer提升约束优化效率，实现快速求解。



摘要： 本文提出PolyFormer，一种将物理和几何知识直接用于简化优化问题的新方法。通过捕捉约束背后的几何结构并将其转化为高效的多面体形式，PolyFormer有效降低了问题复杂度，使传统优化求解器能高效生成可行解。在资源聚合、网络约束优化和不确定性优化等任务中，PolyFormer实现了高达6400倍的计算加速和99.87%的内存减少，同时保持了与先进方法相当或更优的解质量。该方法为科学发现和工程应用中的可扩展约束优化提供了高效可靠的解决方案。

item.title: 基于神经科学的视觉-语言-动作系统设计与优化
item.content:encoded:
提出一种神经科学启发的视觉-语言-动作系统框架。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于神经科学原理的视觉-语言-动作系统设计，包括大脑皮层、桥脑和小脑的模块化结构。该系统通过固定比例调度和两阶段特征缓存实现计算高效性和可重复性。研究展示了在LIBERO数据集上的初步实验结果，显示特征缓存分割可显著减少训练时间并提高成功率，SaiVLA0模型达到99.0%的平均成功率。

item.title: 基于测试时自适应的在线类别发现方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新框架提升在线类别发现效果。



摘要： 本文针对在线类别发现（OCD）中现有方法的局限性，提出了一种测试时自适应框架。该框架通过语义感知原型更新和稳定测试时编码器更新，实现模型在发现新类别时的持续学习。同时，在离线阶段引入边界感知的logit校准，增强类间差异并提升类内紧凑性，为未来类别发现预留嵌入空间。实验表明，该方法在标准基准上显著优于现有哈希方法，有效提升了新类别识别准确率并缓解了类别爆炸问题。

item.title: 跨持久图密度分析与机器学习应用研究
item.content:encoded:
研究跨持久图密度及其在点云区分中的应用。



摘要： 本文首次系统研究了跨持久图（cross-persistence diagrams）的密度，证明了其存在性并建立了统计基础。作者设计了一个机器学习框架，直接从点云坐标和距离矩阵预测跨持久图密度。该方法利用跨持久图的线性特性，有效区分来自不同流形的点云数据。实验表明，引入噪声可以提升区分能力，且该方法在多种数据集上优于现有技术。研究为跨持久图的应用提供了新方向，有望拓展至时间序列和AI生成文本的几何分析。

item.title: 基于边界感知的流式TTS优化方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种流式TTS优化策略，提升语音合成质量与稳定性。



摘要： 本文针对流式文本语音合成（Streaming TTS）面临的韵律不自然和长文本崩溃问题，提出了一种基于韵律边界感知的后训练策略。该方法通过弱时间对齐数据微调预训练的LLM-TTS模型，使模型在有限未来文本下能提前停止。推理阶段采用滑动窗口提示，保持上下文受限并实现无缝拼接。实验表明，该方法在短文本和长文本合成中均优于现有基线，特别是在长文本合成中，词错误率降低了66.2%，同时提升了说话人和情感相似度。

item.title: 量子机器学习中的频谱表达增强方法
item.content:encoded:
量子模型通过残差学习提升多频成分学习能力。



摘要： 本文研究了基于参数化电路的量子机器学习模型在学习多频率函数时的局限性，提出通过残差学习方法迭代训练额外量子模块以提升模型对高频或非主导频率的捕捉能力。实验表明，残差学习显著提升了模型的测试均方误差，且量子比特数量、编码方案和残差结构对多频解析至关重要。该工作为增强量子模型的频谱表达能力提供了实用框架，并揭示了其频率学习行为的新见解。

item.title: 结构化通用人工智能理论SMGI的提出与分析
item.content:encoded:
SMGI提出了一种结构化通用人工智能理论，强调学习接口的动态演化。



摘要： 本文提出了SMGI（结构化通用人工智能）理论，将传统学习问题从固定环境中的假设优化重新定义为学习接口的受控演化。通过引入一个类型化的元模型θ，该模型将表示映射、假设空间、结构先验、多模式评估器和记忆操作器作为动态组件进行形式化。文章定义了通用人工智能为满足四类条件的耦合动力系统(θ, T_θ)，包括结构闭包、动态稳定性、有限统计能力以及评估不变性。同时，证明了一个结构化泛化边界，连接了序列PAC-Bayes分析与Lyapunov稳定性，并展示了经典方法如经验风险最小化、强化学习等均为SMGI的结构受限实例。

item.title: VisionCreator-R1：一种具有显式反思机制的视觉生成代理
item.content:encoded:
提出具备反思机制的视觉生成代理VisionCreator-R1，提升多图像生成效果。



摘要： 文章介绍了VisionCreator-R1，这是一种具有显式反思机制的视觉生成代理，旨在解决现有生成模型在多图像工作流中缺乏系统性反思和纠错能力的问题。通过引入Reflection-Plan Co-Optimization (RPCO) 训练方法，该模型在自建数据集VCR-SFT和VCR-RL上进行训练与优化，有效提升了生成质量。实验结果显示，VisionCreator-R1在多个基准测试中表现优于Gemini2.5Pro，并在单图与多图任务中均展现出优越性能。

item.title: Backdoor4Good：利用后门机制提升大语言模型的可信性
item.content:encoded:
后门机制可用于提升模型安全性与可控性，而非仅作为威胁。



摘要： 本文提出 Backdoor4Good (B4G)，一个统一的基准和框架，用于在大语言模型中实现有益的后门应用。不同于传统研究关注攻击与防御，B4G 将后门机制用于增强安全性和可控制性。该框架基于三元组形式 (T, A, U)，分别表示触发器、激活机制和效用函数，并涵盖四个以信任为核心的用途。实验表明，有益后门在保持任务性能的同时，具备高可控性、抗篡改性和隐蔽性。研究揭示后门不一定是恶意的，合理设计下可成为可信 AI 系统的重要组成部分。

item.title: MM-CondChain：面向多模态大语言模型的深度组合推理基准
item.content:encoded:
提出MM-CondChain，评估MLLM在视觉组合条件下的深度推理能力。



摘要： 本文提出了MM-CondChain，一个用于评估多模态大语言模型（MLLM）在视觉基础上进行深度组合推理能力的基准。该基准通过多层推理链组织每个实例，每层包含基于视觉证据的复杂组合条件。为构建此类数据，作者设计了代理合成流程，包括规划器、可验证程序中间表示（VPIR）和合成器。实验表明，即使最强的模型也仅达到53.33的Path F1分数，且在复杂任务中表现显著下降，证明深度组合推理仍是重大挑战。

item.title: 基于动态代理的图像去雨方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出Derain-Agent框架提升图像去雨效果。



摘要： 尽管深度学习在单图像去雨方面取得进展，但现有模型因采用静态推理方式，难以应对真实场景中的复杂退化问题，导致复原图像存在残留伪影和感知质量不一致。本文提出Derain-Agent，一种可插拔的精修框架，将去雨过程从静态处理转向动态代理式恢复。该框架通过规划网络智能调度修复工具序列，并利用强度调节机制实现空间自适应应用，从而在不增加计算成本的情况下精准修正残余错误。实验表明，该方法在合成与真实数据集上均显著提升了先进去雨模型的性能。

item.title: MedPruner：一种高效的3D医学图像理解令牌剪枝框架
item.content:encoded:
MedPruner提升3D医学图像处理效率，减少计算负担。



摘要： 本文提出MedPruner，一种无需训练且适用于多种模型的分层令牌剪枝框架，用于提升3D医学图像理解的效率。该框架通过两阶段机制：首先利用基于切片锚点的过滤模块消除时间冗余，再通过动态信息核心选择策略实现自适应令牌压缩。实验表明，MedPruner在多个3D医学基准和模型上显著减少冗余令牌，使MedGemma等模型在保留少于5%视觉令牌的情况下仍保持或超越原性能，大幅降低计算开销，验证了动态令牌选择在临床部署中的必要性。

item.title: JAX中自缩放布罗伊登族拟牛顿方法的实现
item.content:encoded:
提供JAX兼容的自缩放布罗伊登族优化器实现。



摘要： 本文介绍了在JAX框架下实现的自缩放布罗伊登族拟牛顿方法，包括BFGS、DFP、Broyden及其自缩放变体（SSBFGS、SSDFP、SSBroyden），并集成满足强 Wolfe 条件的 Zoom 线搜索。该技术笔记旨在记录实现细节，便于JAX社区采用这些优化器，但不声称任何新贡献。代码已开源。

item.title: 大型语言模型在安全补丁生成中的有效性分析
item.content:encoded:
LLM生成的安全补丁有效性低，需严格验证。



摘要： 本研究分析了319个由大型语言模型生成的Java安全补丁，涉及Vul4J基准中的64个漏洞。通过编译性、安全性（通过PoV测试）和功能性的三轴评估发现，仅有24.8%的补丁完全正确，51.4%同时失败于安全性和功能性。主要失败原因是语义理解错误，即生成的代码语法正确但修复策略错误。研究提出的SRS评分显示，LLMs在保持功能方面表现较好（平均0.832），但在安全性方面较差（平均0.251）。不同类型的漏洞修复难度差异显著，输入验证漏洞修复率为0%，而无限循环漏洞修复率最高为45%。研究强调，LLM生成的安全补丁在部署前需经过严格验证。

item.title: 基于方差感知的扩散模型训练优化方法
item.content:encoded:
通过方差感知自适应加权提升扩散模型训练稳定性与生成性能。



摘要： 本文研究了扩散模型在不同噪声水平下的训练动态不平衡问题，从损失方差角度分析并提出一种基于方差感知的自适应加权策略。该方法根据方差分布动态调整训练权重，实现噪声水平间的平衡优化。实验表明，该方法在CIFAR-10和CIFAR-100数据集上显著提升了生成性能，降低了FID分数，并减少了随机种子间的性能波动。通过损失-对数SNR可视化、方差热图和消融实验进一步验证了该方法的有效性，证明了方差感知训练策略在改进扩散模型优化中的潜力。

item.title: 基于视觉语言模型的文本到点云定位方法
item.content:encoded:
提出VLM-Loc框架提升文本到点云定位精度。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于视觉语言模型（VLM）的文本到点云定位方法VLM-Loc，通过将点云转换为鸟瞰图和场景图，结合几何与语义信息，增强空间推理能力。引入部分节点分配机制，实现文本与场景图节点的显式关联，提高定位准确性。构建CityLoc基准数据集用于评估，实验表明VLM-Loc在复杂环境中表现优于现有方法。

item.title: 基于扩散模型的Rb-82动态心脏PET去噪方法DECADE
item.content:encoded:
DECADE提升Rb-82 PET图像质量，无需配对数据。



摘要： 本文提出DECADE，一种用于Rb-82动态心脏PET去噪的无监督扩散框架。该方法通过时间一致性设计，在不依赖配对清洁与噪声数据的情况下，有效降低噪声并保持心肌血流和储备的定量准确性。在Siemens Vision 450和Biograph Vision Quadra设备的数据集上验证，DECADE在动态和参数图像质量以及K1/MBF量化方面优于现有方法，为临床诊断提供更清晰、可靠的影像支持。

item.title: 向量球张量乘积的积分公式与应用
item.content:encoded:
简化向量球张量乘积，提升SO(3)等变神经网络效率。



摘要： 本文推导了Xie等人提出的向量球张量乘积的积分公式，该乘积将Gaunt张量乘积推广至反对称耦合。我们获得了反对称Gaunt系数的显式闭式表达式，从而可以用单个向量球张量乘积模拟Clebsch-Gordan张量乘积，使所需张量乘积计算量减少9倍。研究还探讨了Gaunt和向量球张量乘积在控制表达性与运行时间权衡中的作用，并分析了其归一化低秩分解，为等变神经网络的应用提供了高效实现路径。

item.title: 基于自由文本的结构化放射报告生成方法 ProtoSR
item.content:encoded:
ProtoSR 利用自由文本提升结构化报告准确性。



摘要： 文章提出 ProtoSR 方法，通过从自由文本报告中提取信息，增强结构化报告的生成效果。该方法利用大规模数据构建多模态知识库，并通过视觉原型增强模型预测，显著提升了对细节属性的识别能力。在 Rad-ReStruct 基准测试中，ProtoSR 表现出色，尤其在细粒度问题上效果最佳。

item.title: 基于结构化解释的深度学习模型可解释性框架I2X
item.content:encoded:
I2X框架通过结构化解释提升模型透明度与预测性能。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为I2X的深度学习模型可解释性框架，旨在解决现有方法依赖辅助模型导致的不忠实问题。I2X通过从后验XAI方法中提取原型，并在训练过程中量化关键检查点的进展，构建结构化的解释。该框架不仅揭示了图像分类模型的原型推理过程，还能通过识别不确定原型并进行样本扰动优化，提升不同模型架构和数据集上的预测准确率。I2X既忠实解释模型行为，又为模型优化提供了实用路径。

item.title: CLIPO：提升大语言模型推理能力的对比学习方法
item.content:encoded:
CLIPO通过对比学习提升LLM推理鲁棒性与泛化能力。



摘要： 本文提出CLIPO方法，通过引入对比学习机制改进基于可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR），以解决仅依赖最终答案导致的推理步骤错误问题。CLIPO通过优化成功轨迹上的对比损失，使模型捕捉正确推理路径中的不变结构，从而增强跨轨迹的正则化效果，有效减少推理不一致和幻觉现象。实验表明，CLIPO在多个推理基准上均优于现有RLVR基线，显著提升了LLM的泛化能力和鲁棒性。

item.title: InSpatio-WorldFM：一种实时空间智能的开源帧模型
item.content:encoded:
InSpatio-WorldFM实现低延迟实时空间推理。



摘要： InSpatio-WorldFM是一种开源的实时帧模型，用于空间智能。与依赖顺序帧生成的视频世界模型不同，它采用帧级独立生成方式，减少延迟，提高实时性。通过显式3D锚点和隐式空间记忆确保多视角一致性，保持全局场景几何和细节。研究还提出一个渐进式三阶段训练流程，将预训练图像扩散模型转化为可控帧模型，并最终成为实时生成器。实验表明，该模型在消费级GPU上实现强多视角一致性和交互探索，为实时世界模拟提供高效替代方案。

item.content:encoded:
随机平滑提升轨迹预测模型抗攻击能力。

item.title: 基于数据驱动的激光点火火箭发动机仿真方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种数据驱动的代理模型，提升火箭发动机仿真效率。



摘要： 文章提出一种结合卷积自编码器和神经微分方程的数据驱动代理建模方法，用于高效模拟激光点火火箭发动机的复杂物理过程。该方法通过压缩高维流场数据到低维潜在空间，并利用神经微分方程学习系统时间演化，从而大幅降低仿真计算成本，实现对输入参数空间的高效探索与不确定性量化。该方法为实时数字孪生技术提供了新路径，并推动了代理模型在复杂系统中的应用。

item.title: 4DEquine: Disentangling Motion and Appearance for 4D Equine Reconstruction from Monocular Video
item.content:encoded: 4D reconstruction of equine family (e.g. horses) from monocular video is important for animal welfare. Previous mainstream 4D animal reconstruction methods require joint optimization of motion and appearance over a whole video, which is time-consuming and sensitive to incomplete observation. In this work, we propose a novel framework called 4DEquine by disentangling the 4D reconstruction problem into two sub-problems: dynamic motion reconstruction and static appearance reconstruction. For motion, we introduce a simple yet effective spatio-temporal transformer with a post-optimization stage to regress smooth and pixel-aligned pose and shape sequences from video. For appearance, we design a novel feed-forward network that reconstructs a high-fidelity, animatable 3D Gaussian avatar from as few as a single image. To assist training, we create a large-scale synthetic motion dataset, VarenPoser, which features high-quality surface motions and diverse camera trajectories, as well as a synthetic appearance dataset, VarenTex, comprising realistic multi-view images generated through multi-view diffusion. While training only on synthetic datasets, 4DEquine achieves state-of-the-art performance on real-world APT36K and AiM datasets, demonstrating the superiority of 4DEquine and our new datasets for both geometry and appearance reconstruction. Comprehensive ablation studies validate the effectiveness of both the motion and appearance reconstruction network. Project page: https://luoxue-star.github.io/4DEquine_Project_Page/.

item.title: Reinforcement learning-based dynamic cleaning scheduling framework for solar energy system
item.content:encoded: Advancing autonomous green technologies in solar photovoltaic (PV) systems is key to improving sustainability and efficiency in renewable energy production. This study presents a reinforcement learning (RL)-based framework to autonomously optimize the cleaning schedules of PV panels in arid regions, where soiling from dust and other airborne particles significantly reduces energy output. By employing advanced RL algorithms, Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) and Soft Actor-Critic (SAC), the framework dynamically adjusts cleaning intervals based on uncertain environmental conditions. The proposed approach was applied to a case study in Abu Dhabi, UAE, demonstrating that PPO outperformed SAC and traditional simulation optimization (Sim-Opt) methods, achieving up to 13% cost savings by dynamically responding to weather uncertainties. The results highlight the superiority of flexible, autonomous scheduling over fixed-interval methods, particularly in adapting to stochastic environmental dynamics. This aligns with the goals of autonomous green energy production by reducing operational costs and improving the efficiency of solar power generation systems. This work underscores the potential of RL-driven autonomous decision-making to optimize maintenance operations in renewable energy systems. In future research, it is important to enhance the generalization ability of the proposed RL model, while also considering additional factors and constraints to apply it to different regions.

item.title: "Better Ask for Forgiveness than Permission": Practices and Policies of AI Disclosure in Freelance Work
item.content:encoded: The growing use of AI applications among freelance workers is reshaping trust and relationships with clients. This paper investigates how both workers and clients perceive AI use and disclosure in the freelance economy through a three-stage study: interviews with workers and two survey studies with workers and clients. Findings first reveal a key expectation gap around disclosure: Workers often adopt passive disclosure practices, revealing AI use only when asked, as they assume clients can already detect it. Clients, however, are far less confident in recognizing AI-assisted work and prefer proactive disclosure. A second finding highlights the role of unclear or absent client AI policies, which leave workers consistently misinterpreting clients' expectations for AI use and disclosure. Together, these gaps point to the need for clearer guidelines and practices for AI disclosure. Insights extend beyond freelancing, offering implications for trust, accountability, and policy design in other AI-mediated work domains.

item.title: From Word2Vec to Transformers: Text-Derived Composition Embeddings for Filtering Combinatorial Electrocatalysts
item.content:encoded: Compositionally complex solid solution electrocatalysts span vast composition spaces, and even one materials system can contain more candidate compositions than can be measured exhaustively. Here we evaluate a label-free screening strategy that represents each composition using embeddings derived from scientific texts and prioritizes candidates based on similarity to two property concepts. We compare a corpus-trained Word2Vec baseline with transformer-based embeddings, where compositions are encoded either by linear element-wise mixing or by short composition prompts. Similarities to `concept directions', the terms conductivity and dielectric, define a 2-dimensional descriptor space, and a symmetric Pareto-front selection is used to filter candidate subsets without using electrochemical labels. Performance is assessed on 15 materials libraries including noble metal alloys and multicomponent oxides. In this setting, the lightweight Word2Vec baseline, which uses a simple linear combination of element embeddings, often achieves the highest number of reductions of possible candidate compositions while staying close to the best measured performance.

item.title: Bio-Inspired Self-Supervised Learning for Wrist-worn IMU Signals
item.content:encoded: Wearable accelerometers have enabled large-scale health and wellness monitoring, yet learning robust human-activity representations has been constrained by the scarcity of labeled data. While self-supervised learning offers a potential remedy, existing approaches treat sensor streams as unstructured time series, overlooking the underlying biological structure of human movement, a factor we argue is critical for effective Human Activity Recognition (HAR). We introduce a novel tokenization strategy grounded in the submovement theory of motor control, which posits that continuous wrist motion is composed of superposed elementary basis functions called submovements. We define our token as the movement segment, a unit of motion composed of a finite sequence of submovements that is readily extractable from wrist accelerometer signals. By treating these segments as tokens, we pretrain a Transformer encoder via masked movement-segment reconstruction to model the temporal dependencies of movement segments, shifting the learning focus beyond local waveform morphology. Pretrained on the NHANES corpus (approximately 28k hours; approximately 11k participants; approximately 10M windows), our representations outperform strong wearable SSL baselines across six subject-disjoint HAR benchmarks. Furthermore, they demonstrate stronger data efficiency in data-scarce settings. Code and pretrained weights will be made publicly available.

item.title: Prompt-Based Caption Generation for Single-Tooth Dental Images Using Vision-Language Models
item.content:encoded: Digital dentistry has made significant advances with the advent of deep learning. However, the majority of these deep learning-based dental image analysis models focus on very specific tasks such as tooth segmentation, tooth detection, cavity detection, and gingivitis classification. There is a lack of a specialized model that has holistic knowledge of teeth and can perform dental image analysis tasks based on that knowledge. Datasets of dental images with captions can help build such a model. To the best of our knowledge, existing dental image datasets with captions are few in number and limited in scope. In many of these datasets, the captions describe the entire mouth, while the images are limited to the anterior view. As a result, posterior teeth such as molars are not clearly visible, limiting the usefulness of the captions for training vision-language models. Additionally, the captions focus only on a specific disease (gingivitis) and do not provide a holistic assessment of each tooth. Moreover, tooth disease scores are typically assigned to individual teeth, and each tooth is treated as a separate entity in orthodontic procedures. Therefore, it is important to have captions for single-tooth images. As far as we know, no such dataset of single-tooth images with dental captions exists. In this work, we aim to bridge that gap by assessing the possibility of generating captions for dental images using Vision-Language Models (VLMs) and evaluating the extent and quality of those captions. Our findings suggest that guided prompts help VLMs generate meaningful captions. We show that the prompts generated by our framework are better anchored in describing the visual aspects of dental images. We selected RGB images as they have greater potential in consumer scenarios.

item.title: PONTE: Personalized Orchestration for Natural Language Trustworthy Explanations
item.content:encoded: Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) seeks to enhance the transparency and accountability of machine learning systems, yet most methods follow a one-size-fits-all paradigm that neglects user differences in expertise, goals, and cognitive needs. Although Large Language Models can translate technical explanations into natural language, they introduce challenges related to faithfulness and hallucinations. To address these challenges, we present PONTE (Personalized Orchestration for Natural language Trustworthy Explanations), a human-in-the-loop framework for adaptive and reliable XAI narratives. PONTE models personalization as a closed-loop validation and adaptation process rather than prompt engineering. It combines: (i) a low-dimensional preference model capturing stylistic requirements; (ii) a preference-conditioned generator grounded in structured XAI artifacts; and (iii) verification modules enforcing numerical faithfulness, informational completeness, and stylistic alignment, optionally supported by retrieval-grounded argumentation. User feedback iteratively updates the preference state, enabling quick personalization. Automatic and human evaluations across healthcare and finance domains show that the verification-refinement loop substantially improves completeness and stylistic alignment over validation-free generation. Human studies further confirm strong agreement between intended preference vectors and perceived style, robustness to generation stochasticity, and consistently positive quality assessments.

item.title: Separable neural architectures as a primitive for unified predictive and generative intelligence
item.content:encoded: Intelligent systems across physics, language and perception often exhibit factorisable structure, yet are typically modelled by monolithic neural architectures that do not explicitly exploit this structure. The separable neural architecture (SNA) addresses this by formalising a representational class that unifies additive, quadratic and tensor-decomposed neural models. By constraining interaction order and tensor rank, SNAs impose a structural inductive bias that factorises high-dimensional mappings into low-arity components. Separability need not be a property of the system itself: it often emerges in the coordinates or representations through which the system is expressed. Crucially, this coordinate-aware formulation reveals a structural analogy between chaotic spatiotemporal dynamics and linguistic autoregression. By treating continuous physical states as smooth, separable embeddings, SNAs enable distributional modelling of chaotic systems. This approach mitigates the nonphysical drift characteristics of deterministic operators whilst remaining applicable to discrete sequences. The compositional versatility of this approach is demonstrated across four domains: autonomous waypoint navigation via reinforcement learning, inverse generation of multifunctional microstructures, distributional modelling of turbulent flow and neural language modelling. These results establish the separable neural architecture as a domain-agnostic primitive for predictive and generative intelligence, capable of unifying both deterministic and distributional representations.

item.title: $Δ$VLA: Prior-Guided Vision-Language-Action Models via World Knowledge Variation
item.content:encoded: Recent vision-language-action (VLA) models have significantly advanced robotic manipulation by unifying perception, reasoning, and control. To achieve such integration, recent studies adopt a predictive paradigm that models future visual states or world knowledge to guide action generation. However, these models emphasize forecasting outcomes rather than reasoning about the underlying process of change, which is essential for determining how to act. To address this, we propose $Δ$VLA, a prior-guided framework that models world-knowledge variations relative to an explicit current-world knowledge prior for action generation, rather than regressing absolute future world states. Specifically, 1) to construct the current world knowledge prior, we propose the Prior-Guided WorldKnowledge Extractor (PWKE). It extracts manipulable regions, spatial relations, and semantic cues from the visual input, guided by auxiliary heads and prior pseudo labels, thus reducing redundancy. 2) Building upon this, to represent how world knowledge evolves under actions, we introduce the Latent World Variation Quantization (LWVQ). It learns a discrete latent space via a VQ-VAE objective to encode world knowledge variations, shifting prediction from full modalities to compact latent. 3)Moreover, to mitigate interference during variation modeling, we design the Conditional Variation Attention (CV-Atten), whichpromotes disentangled learning and preserves the independence of knowledge representations. Extensive experiments on both simulated benchmarks and real-world robotic tasks demonstrate $Δ$VLA achieves state-of-the-art performance while improving efficiency. Code and real-world execution videos are available at https://github.com/JiuTian-VL/DeltaVLA.

item.title: InFusionLayer: a CFA-based ensemble tool to generate new classifiers for learning and modeling
item.content:encoded: Ensemble learning is a well established body of methods for machine learning to enhance predictive performance by combining multiple algorithms/models. Combinatorial Fusion Analysis (CFA) has provided method and practice for combining multiple scoring systems, using rank-score characteristic (RSC) function and cognitive diversity (CD), including ensemble method and model fusion. However, there is no general-purpose Python tool available that incorporate these techniques. In this paper we introduce \texttt{InFusionLayer}, a machine learning architecture inspired by CFA at the system fusion level that uses a moderate set of base models to optimize unsupervised and supervised learning multiclassification problems. We demonstrate \texttt{InFusionLayer}'s ease of use for PyTorch, TensorFlow, and Scikit-learn workflows by validating its performance on various computer vision datasets. Our results highlight the practical advantages of incorporating distinctive features of RSC function and CD, paving the way for more sophisticated ensemble learning applications in machine learning. We open-sourced our code to encourage continuing development and community accessibility to leverage CFA on github: https://github.com/ewroginek/Infusion

item.title: A Bipartite Graph Approach to U.S.-China Cross-Market Return Forecasting
item.content:encoded: This paper studies cross-market return predictability through a machine learning framework that preserves economic structure. Exploiting the non-overlapping trading hours of the U.S. and Chinese equity markets, we construct a directed bipartite graph that captures time-ordered predictive linkages between stocks across markets. Edges are selected via rolling-window hypothesis testing, and the resulting graph serves as a sparse, economically interpretable feature-selection layer for downstream machine learning models. We apply a range of regularized and ensemble methods to forecast open-to-close returns using lagged foreign-market information. Our results reveal a pronounced directional asymmetry: U.S. previous-close-to-close returns contain substantial predictive information for Chinese intraday returns, whereas the reverse effect is limited. This informational asymmetry translates into economically meaningful performance differences and highlights how structured machine learning frameworks can uncover cross-market dependencies while maintaining interpretability.

item.title: Cognitively Layered Data Synthesis for Domain Adaptation of LLMs to Space Situational Awareness
item.content:encoded: Large language models (LLMs) demonstrate exceptional performance on general-purpose tasks. however, transferring them to complex engineering domains such as space situational awareness (SSA) remains challenging owing to insufficient structural alignment with mission chains, the absence of higher-order cognitive supervision, and poor correspondence between data quality criteria and engineering specifications. The core bottleneck is the construction of high-quality supervised fine-tuning (SFT) datasets. To this end, we propose BD-FDG (Bloom's Taxonomy-based Domain-specific Fine-tuning Data Generation), a framework that addresses incomplete knowledge coverage, shallow cognitive depth, and limited quality controllability through three mechanisms: structured knowledge organization, cognitively layered question modeling, and automated quality control. The framework uses a knowledge tree to ensure structured corpus coverage, designs a question generation scheme spanning nine categories and six cognitive levels from Remember to Create to produce samples with a continuous difficulty gradient, and applies a multidimensional scoring pipeline to enforce domain rigor and consistency. Using BD-FDG, we construct SSA-SFT, a domain dataset of approximately 230K samples, and fine-tune Qwen3-8B to obtain SSA-LLM-8B. Experiments show that SSA-LLM-8B achieves relative BLEU-1 improvements of 144\% (no-think) and 176\% (think) on the domain test set and a win rate of 82.21\% over the baseline in arena comparisons, while largely preserving general benchmark performance (MMLU-Pro, MATH-500). These results validate SFT data construction driven by cognitive layering as an effective paradigm for complex engineering domains and provide a transferable framework for domain-specific LLM adaptation.

item.title: NN-OpInf: an operator inference approach using structure-preserving composable neural networks
item.content:encoded: We propose neural network operator inference (NN-OpInf): a structure-preserving, composable, and minimally restrictive operator inference framework for the non-intrusive reduced-order modeling of dynamical systems. The approach learns latent dynamics from snapshot data, enforcing local operator structure such as skew-symmetry, (semi-)positive definiteness, and gradient preservation, while also reflecting complex dynamics by supporting additive compositions of heterogeneous operators. We present practical training strategies and analyze computational costs relative to linear and quadratic polynomial OpInf (P-OpInf). Numerical experiments across several nonlinear and parametric problems demonstrate improved accuracy, stability, and robustness over P-OpInf and prior NN-ROM formulations, particularly when the dynamics are not well represented by polynomial models. These results suggest that NN-OpInf can serve as an effective drop-in replacement for P-OpInf when the dynamics to be modeled contain non-polynomial nonlinearities, offering potential gains in accuracy and out-of-distribution performance at the expense of higher training computational costs and a more difficult, non-convex learning problem.

item.title: Characterization and upgrade of a quantum graph neural network for charged particle tracking
item.content:encoded: In the forthcoming years the LHC experiments are going to be upgraded to benefit from the substantial increase of the LHC instantaneous luminosity, which will lead to larger, denser events, and, consequently, greater complexity in reconstructing charged particle tracks, motivating frontier research in new technologies. Quantum machine learning models are being investigated as potential new approaches to high energy physics (HEP) tasks. We characterize and upgrade a quantum graph neural network (QGNN) architecture for charged particle track reconstruction on a simulated high luminosity dataset. The model operates on a set of event graphs, each built from the hits generated in tracking detector layers by particles produced in proton collisions, performing a classification of the possible hit connections between adjacent layers. In this approach the QGNN is designed as a hybrid architecture, interleaving classical feedforward networks with parametrized quantum circuits. We characterize the interplay between the classical and quantum components. We report on the principal upgrades to the original design, and present new evidence of improved training behavior, specifically in terms of convergence toward the final trained configuration.

item.title: 基于多维情感信号的原油价格预测研究
item.content:encoded:
多维情感信号提升原油期货收益预测准确性。



摘要： 本文研究了利用大语言模型提取的多维情感信号是否能提高周度WTI原油期货收益的预测能力。通过2020年至2025年的能源行业新闻文章，构建了涵盖相关性、极性、强度、不确定性和前瞻性五个维度的情感指标，并结合GPT-4o、Llama 3.2-3b、FinBERT和AlphaVantage等模型进行分析。结果表明，将GPT-4o与FinBERT结合可获得最佳预测效果，说明大语言模型与传统金融情感模型具有互补性。SHAP分析进一步显示，强度和不确定性特征是关键预测因子，表明新闻情感的预测价值超越了简单极性分析。研究结果表明，多维情感测量有助于提升商品收益预测和能源市场风险监控。

item.title: 基于注意力机制的后门样本检测方法BackdoorIDS
item.content:encoded:
提出一种无需训练的后门样本检测方法BackdoorIDS。



摘要： 本文提出BackdoorIDS，一种无需训练、在推理阶段即可检测预训练视觉编码器中后门样本的方法。该方法基于注意力劫持和恢复现象，在逐步输入掩码过程中，后门图像会先关注恶意触发特征，随后注意力转移至正常内容，导致嵌入向量发生显著变化。通过密度聚类分析嵌入序列，若形成多个簇则判定为后门样本。实验表明，BackdoorIDS在多种攻击类型、数据集和模型架构中均表现优异，且无需重新训练，具有良好的兼容性。

item.title: 基于Transformer的DeepHistoViT在组织病理学图像分类中的应用
item.content:encoded:
DeepHistoViT在癌症组织病理学图像分类中表现优异。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于Transformer的DeepHistoViT框架，用于自动化分类组织病理学图像。该模型通过集成注意力机制，能够捕捉细粒度的细胞结构，并提高诊断相关区域的可解释性。在三个公开数据集（肺癌、结肠癌和急性淋巴细胞白血病）上进行了评估，结果显示其分类准确率、精确率、召回率、F1分数和ROC-AUC均达到或接近100%。这些结果表明，Transformer架构在组织病理学图像分析中具有显著优势，DeepHistoViT有望作为辅助病理诊断工具，提升临床决策效率。

item.title: 新型特征提取库Nyxus提升大规模图像数据处理效率
item.content:encoded:
Nyxus提升图像数据分析效率与可扩展性。



摘要： 现代成像设备产生的海量图像数据对计算能力提出了更高要求。传统图像分析算法在处理大规模数据时效率不足，且难以在准确性和鲁棒性之间取得平衡。深度学习虽提升了区域分割的准确性，但各领域特征提取工具的多样化使性能比较变得复杂。为此，研究者开发了名为Nyxus的新特征提取库，该库专为2D和3D图像数据设计，支持跨CPU和GPU的高效计算，并覆盖多个生物医学领域。Nyxus提供了多种使用方式，包括Python包、命令行工具、Napari插件及OCI容器，适用于不同技能水平的用户。此外，Nyxus还支持程序化调整特征集，以优化计算效率或覆盖范围，适用于新型机器学习和深度学习应用。

item.title: 基于Transformer的多变量时间序列概率预测框架EnTransformer
item.content:encoded:
EnTransformer通过引入随机噪声和能量评分目标，提升多变量时间序列预测性能。



摘要： 本文提出EnTransformer，一种结合生成模型与Transformer架构的多变量时间序列概率预测框架。该方法通过注入随机噪声并优化能量评分目标，直接学习条件预测分布，无需参数假设。EnTransformer在多个基准数据集上表现优异，能够生成一致的多变量预测轨迹，并有效捕捉长时序依赖和跨序列交互。实验结果表明，该模型在概率预测校准性和准确性方面均优于现有基准模型。

item.title: ELIT：一种可动态调整计算资源的扩散Transformer机制
item.content:encoded:
ELIT提升生成质量并优化计算资源分配。



摘要： 本文提出ELIT，一种与扩散Transformer（DiT）兼容的机制，通过引入可学习的潜在接口序列，实现输入图像大小与计算量的解耦。ELIT利用轻量级读写交叉注意力层，将信息在空间标记和潜在标记之间传递，并优先处理重要区域。训练中通过随机丢弃尾部潜在标记，使模型生成按重要性排序的表示。推理时可根据计算约束动态调整潜在标记数量，从而提升生成质量并优化资源分配。实验显示，ELIT在多个数据集和架构上均取得显著提升。

item.title: 多模态基准数据集CoMMET评估大语言模型的心智理论能力
item.content:encoded:
提出多模态基准CoMMET评估LLM的心智理论能力。



摘要： 本文提出了一种新的多模态基准数据集CoMMET，用于评估大型语言模型（LLMs）在心智理论（ToM）方面的能力。该数据集扩展了传统评测范围，涵盖更广泛的心理状态，并引入多轮对话测试，是首个在多轮对话场景中评估ToM的多模态数据集。通过全面评估不同家族和规模的LLMs，研究分析了现有模型的优势与不足，并指出了未来改进方向，有助于深入理解现代LLMs的社会认知能力。

item.title: 基于风险可控的多视角驾驶场景生成方法
item.content:encoded:
提出RiskMV-DPO方法，实现高风险驾驶场景的自动生成。



摘要： 本文提出RiskMV-DPO，一种物理驱动、风险可控的多视角驾驶场景生成框架。该方法通过整合目标风险等级与物理风险建模，自动生成多样化且高风险的动态轨迹，作为扩散视频生成的几何锚点。为保证时空一致性和几何真实性，引入了几何-外观对齐模块和区域感知直接偏好优化策略。实验表明，RiskMV-DPO在nuScenes数据集上显著提升了3D检测性能并改善了视觉质量，为具身智能的安全开发提供了可扩展工具链。

item.title: 基于神经网络的高维非线性结构系统不确定性建模研究
item.content:encoded:
提出三种元模型框架用于处理结构系统中的不确定性。



摘要： 本文针对高维非线性动态结构系统在自然灾害下的不确定性建模问题，提出了三种元模型框架，分别结合多层感知机（MLP）、消息传递神经网络（MPNN）和自编码器（AE）与长短期记忆网络（LSTM）进行结构响应预测。这些框架通过蒙特卡洛dropout和负对数似然损失函数来处理不确定性，并在两个案例研究中验证了其有效性。结果表明，MLP-LSTM在低维系统中表现最佳，而MPNN-LSTM和AE-LSTM在更复杂的钢框架模型中更具优势。同时，预测方差与实际误差之间的一致性表明这些框架适用于主动学习策略和模型置信度评估。

item.title: AutoGaze：提升长时高分辨率视频理解的轻量级模块
item.content:encoded:
AutoGaze通过减少冗余图像块提升视频处理效率和效果。



摘要： 本文提出AutoGaze，一种用于多模态大语言模型的轻量级模块，旨在解决长时高分辨率视频处理中的冗余问题。AutoGaze通过自回归方式选择最少的多尺度图像块，在保证信息完整性的前提下，将视觉标记减少4倍至100倍，使视频处理速度提升最多19倍。该方法在VideoMME等视频基准测试中取得优异成绩，并引入了首个高分辨率、长视频问答基准HLVid，验证了其有效性。

item.title: 基于扩散模型的可组合内在概念提取方法
item.content:encoded:
提出CI-ICE任务与HyperExpress方法，实现图像中可组合概念的准确提取。



摘要： 本文针对无监督概念提取中存在的无法提取可组合内在概念的问题，提出了一个新的任务——可组合且可解释的内在概念提取（CI-ICE）。该任务利用基于扩散的文本到图像模型，从单张图像中提取对象级和属性级的概念，并通过这些概念的组合重建原始概念。为实现这一目标，作者提出了一种名为HyperExpress的方法，该方法通过两个核心方面解决CI-ICE任务：一是利用双曲空间的层次建模能力进行概念解耦，二是引入概念级优化方法以保持复杂的概念间关系并确保概念的可组合性。实验表明，该方法在提取可组合的内在概念方面表现优异。

item.title: 日本病理报告写作中大语言模型的性能评估
item.content:encoded:
评估七种开源大语言模型在病理报告写作中的表现。



摘要： 本文评估了七种开源大语言模型在支持日语病理报告写作中的性能，从三个角度进行分析：（A）按照预定义格式生成和提取病理诊断文本，（B）修正日语病理报告中的拼写错误，（C）由病理科医生和临床医生对模型生成的解释性文本进行主观评价。结果显示，思维型模型和医学专业模型在需要推理的结构化报告任务和拼写修正中表现出优势，但解释性文本的偏好因评估者而异。尽管不同任务中模型的实用性有所差异，研究结果表明开源大语言模型在有限但具有临床意义的场景中可辅助日语病理报告写作。

item.title: FLUX.1中潜在空间颜色表示的解释与控制方法
item.content:encoded:
FLUX.1潜在空间颜色结构可被解释并用于颜色控制。



摘要： 本文研究了FLUX.1变分自编码器潜在空间中的颜色表示，揭示了其反映色相、饱和度和明度的结构。通过验证，该潜在颜色子空间（LCS）能够预测并直接控制图像颜色，提出了一种无需训练的完全基于封闭形式潜在空间操作的方法。该方法为图像生成中的颜色控制提供了新思路，相关代码已公开。

item.title: 基于数字孪生的水下自主对接深度强化学习方法
item.content:encoded:
深度强化学习提升水下自主对接成功率与适应性。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于高保真数字孪生环境的自主水下对接方法，利用Stonefish模拟器构建多进程强化学习框架，加速训练过程并引入真实AUV动力学、碰撞模型和传感器噪声。采用PPO算法训练六自由度控制策略，通过距离、姿态、动作平滑性和自适应碰撞惩罚构建奖励机制。实验表明，在仿真中成功率达到90%以上，并在物理测试中验证了从仿真到现实的迁移有效性，DRL控制器展现出如俯仰制动和偏航振荡等自适应行为。

item.title: 生成式AI技术与社会系统的协同演进研究
item.content:encoded:
生成式AI推动创新，但面临技术、伦理和治理挑战。



摘要： 本文通过系统文献综述分析了2023年以来的28篇相关研究，指出生成式AI虽具有提升生产力和创新的潜力，但其应用受到技术不可靠性、社会伦理风险及治理空白等多重因素的制约。研究强调技术子系统与社会子系统之间的不匹配问题，并提出IS（信息科学）研究应从分析影响转向主动塑造技术与组织流程、社会价值观及监管制度的共同演进，关注人机协作、情境验证、概率系统设计原则及适应性治理。

item.title: 儿童手写动态与学习特征的关系研究
item.content:encoded:
手写动态可反映儿童发展和个体差异，尤其在年级预测中表现显著。



摘要： 本文通过分析日本小学生至初中生的大规模在线手写数据，探讨手写动态是否能编码学生特征。研究比较了三种手写特征：基本统计描述符、基于熵的变异性度量以及sigma-lognormal模型参数。这些特征被用于年级预测、性别分类和学业表现分类任务，并采用线性回归、逻辑回归和随机森林模型进行评估。结果表明，手写动态包含与发育阶段和个体差异相关的信息，尤其是在年级预测任务中表现突出。研究证实了手写运动模式随儿童成长逐渐趋于对数正态分布的组织方式。

item.title: 轻量级实时边缘分割模型PicoSAM3在传感器上的应用
item.content:encoded:
PicoSAM3实现高精度实时边缘分割，适用于智能设备。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种名为PicoSAM3的轻量级可提示视觉分割模型，专为边缘计算和传感器端部署设计。该模型仅包含130万参数，结合密集CNN架构、区域提示编码、高效通道注意力机制，并通过SAM2和SAM3进行知识蒸馏，显著提升了分割性能。在COCO和LVIS数据集上分别达到65.45%和64.01%的mIoU，优于现有同类模型。量化后的INT8版本在Sony IMX500传感器上实现11.82毫秒的实时推理，满足内存和算子限制。实验表明，从大模型蒸馏可提升14.5%的mIoU，证明了在传感器端实现高质量、灵活提示分割的可行性。

item.title: 基于自回归扩散模型的实时人类动画生成方法
item.content:encoded:
提出Neighbor Forcing和ConvKV机制提升实时动画生成效率与质量。



摘要： 本文针对自回归扩散模型在实时人类动画生成中的效率问题，提出了Neighbor Forcing方法，通过传播时间相邻帧作为潜在邻居，实现更稳定的学习信号。同时引入结构化ConvKV记忆机制，压缩注意力键值对，实现常数内存推理和无限视频生成。实验表明该方法在训练收敛性、生成质量和推理效率方面均优于现有方法，支持20 FPS实时流式推理。

item.title: 基于流匹配的虚拟试穿框架PROMO提升图像编辑效率与质量
item.content:encoded:
PROMO通过流匹配技术实现高效高质的虚拟试穿，提升图像编辑性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为PROMO的虚拟试穿框架，采用流匹配DiT架构，并结合潜在多模态条件拼接和自参考机制，有效降低推理开销。该方法在标准基准测试中表现出色，在保持高质量的同时提升了速度，证明了流匹配变压器在虚拟试穿任务中的有效性。PROMO不仅适用于虚拟试穿，还可扩展到更广泛的图像编辑任务，具有良好的通用性和训练效率。

item.title: OmniForcing：实现高效多模态流式生成的扩散模型蒸馏框架
item.content:encoded:
OmniForcing解决多模态扩散模型延迟问题，提升实时生成效率。



摘要： 本文提出OmniForcing，一种将离线双流双向扩散模型蒸馏为高质量流式自回归生成器的框架。针对传统方法在因果蒸馏中因模态时间不对称和令牌稀疏性导致的训练不稳定问题，引入了非对称块因果对齐与零截断全局前缀，以防止多模态同步漂移。同时通过音频Sink Token机制和Identity RoPE约束解决音频令牌稀疏性引发的梯度爆炸问题。最后采用联合自强制蒸馏策略，使模型在长序列生成中动态修正跨模态误差。OmniForcing在单GPU上实现约25 FPS的流式生成，保持与双向教师模型相当的多模态同步性和视觉质量。

item.title: 基于因果自编码与治疗条件的反事实估计方法
item.content:encoded:
CAETC方法提升反事实估计效果，适用于多种时间序列模型。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为因果自编码与治疗条件（CAETC）的新方法，用于解决观测数据中时间依赖性混杂偏差的问题。该方法基于对抗性表示学习，利用自编码架构学习部分可逆且治疗不变的表示，并通过治疗特定条件进行结果预测。该设计不依赖于底层序列模型，可应用于LSTM或TCN等现有架构。实验表明，CAETC在合成、半合成和真实数据上均优于现有方法，显著提升了反事实估计的准确性。

item.title: GraphRAG安全漏洞分析与知识演化污染攻击研究
item.content:encoded:
KEPo攻击方法有效破坏GraphRAG系统安全性。



摘要： 本文针对GraphRAG系统提出了一种新型知识演化污染攻击（KEPo），该方法通过生成有毒事件并伪造知识演化路径，将恶意信息注入知识图谱中，从而误导大语言模型产生有害响应。KEPo在单目标和多目标攻击场景中均表现出色，实验结果表明其攻击成功率显著高于现有方法，揭示了GraphRAG系统的潜在安全风险。

item.title: Video Streaming Thinking：一种实时视频理解的新范式
item.content:encoded:
提出VST范式，实现视频流中同步推理，提升响应效率与理解能力。



摘要： 本文提出Video Streaming Thinking（VST），一种支持边观看边思考的视频理解新范式。该方法在视频流中激活推理机制，通过摊销大语言模型的推理延迟，实现了及时理解和连贯认知，同时保持实时响应。研究还引入了VST-SFT和VST-RL后训练流程，结合视频知识图谱生成高质量的问答对，增强多证据推理和持续注意力。实验表明，VST-7B在多个在线基准测试中表现优异，相比现有方法响应速度提升15.7倍，且在多种视频理解任务中展现出强大泛化能力。

item.title: 基于有效电阻的图神经网络拓扑重连方法
item.content:encoded:
有效电阻重连提升图神经网络长程依赖建模能力。



摘要： 本文针对图神经网络在捕捉长程依赖时面临的过压缩问题，提出一种基于有效电阻的拓扑重连策略（ERR）。该方法通过全局有效电阻检测结构瓶颈，迭代添加高电阻节点对之间的边，同时移除低电阻边，以增强通信路径并控制图密度。实验表明，ERR在保持边数不变的情况下提升了信息传播效率，并与PairNorm等归一化技术结合进一步优化性能。研究还分析了重连对消息传递和嵌入表示演化的影响，揭示了过压缩与过平滑之间的权衡关系。

item.title: 基于神经微分方程的动态场景预测方法
item.content:encoded:
Node-RF通过神经微分方程实现场景动态的长期外推。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为Node-RF的方法，结合神经微分方程（NODEs）和动态神经辐射场（NeRF），以实现对场景动态的连续时间、时空表示。该方法能够在不增加内存成本的情况下，超越观测轨迹进行长期预测。从视觉输入中，Node-RF通过ODE求解器学习隐式场景状态，并通过微积分传播特征嵌入。NeRF渲染器根据计算出的嵌入生成任意视角，实现长距离外推。实验表明，Node-RF可以在不依赖显式模型的情况下表征抽象系统行为，识别未来预测的关键点。

item.title: 基于最小最大优化的鲁棒强化学习方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出MMDDPG方法提升强化学习在干扰下的鲁棒性。



摘要： 本文针对强化学习在面对外部干扰和模型不确定性时性能不稳定的问题，提出了一种基于最小最大优化的深度确定性策略梯度方法（MMDDPG）。该方法通过用户策略与对抗扰动策略之间的博弈，使用户策略在最小化目标函数的同时，对抗策略则最大化该函数。为稳定这种交互，引入了分数目标函数以平衡任务性能和扰动强度，防止过激扰动并促进稳健学习。实验结果表明，MMDDPG在MuJoCo环境中对力扰动和模型参数变化表现出显著增强的鲁棒性。

item.title: 基于3D手部关键点的视角可控视频生成方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新型框架，实现视角可控的高质量视频生成。



摘要： 本文针对虚拟现实和具身AI中的视角可控视频生成问题，提出一种新方法。该方法通过使用稀疏的3D手部关键点作为控制信号，解决现有方法在3D一致性、运动不一致和跨身体泛化方面的不足。研究引入了一个高效的控制模块，能够处理遮挡问题并保留完整的3D信息。同时，构建了一个包含百万级高质量视角视频数据集的自动化标注流程，并建立了跨身体基准测试集。实验表明，该方法在生成高保真视频和跨身体泛化方面均优于现有技术。

item.title: 利用多核CPU并行化解决3D打印对象排列与调度问题
item.content:encoded:
通过并行化算法提升3D打印效率。



摘要： 本文提出一种利用现代多核CPU计算能力来解决顺序3D打印中的对象排列与调度问题的方法。通过将原有CEGAR-SEQ算法并行化，采用多种对象排列策略，如将对象放置在打印板角落或根据高度进行调度，从而提高打印效率。实验表明，该方法Portfolio-CEGAR-SEQ在使用较少打印板的情况下，能够更高效地处理多个打印任务。

item.title: 基于逆向神经算子的隐式ODE参数恢复方法
item.content:encoded:
INO通过两阶段框架高效恢复稀疏观测下的ODE参数。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为逆向神经算子（INO）的两阶段框架，用于从稀疏、部分观测中恢复隐藏的常微分方程（ODE）参数。第一阶段使用带有交叉注意力机制的条件傅里叶神经算子（C-FNO），通过频谱正则化抑制高频伪影，重建完整的ODE轨迹。第二阶段通过摊销漂移模型（ADM）在参数空间中学习加权速度场，将随机参数初始化引导至真实值，避免了梯度反传带来的雅可比不稳定性问题。实验表明，在真实世界的刚性大气化学基准数据（POLLU，25个参数）和合成基因调控网络（GRN，40个参数）上，INO在参数恢复精度上优于基于梯度和摊销的基线方法，且推理时间仅需0.23秒，比迭代梯度下降快487倍。

item.title: 语言模型的正确性偏好源于压缩与一致性原则
item.content:encoded:
语言模型偏好正确陈述，因压缩与一致性原则影响。



摘要： 本文提出“压缩-一致性原则”，解释为何语言模型在训练数据质量混杂时仍偏好正确陈述。研究通过小规模GPT-2风格字符级Transformer模型，在合成数学语料库上测试发现，模型在随机错误设置中强烈倾向于正确补全，准确率高达83.1%。当错误具有逻辑一致性但不正确时，偏好显著减弱。实验还表明，嵌入验证步骤可恢复对正确的偏好，而增加一致规则数量则提升准确性。结果表明，所谓的“真理偏见”更多是压缩压力和内部一致性偏好所致，而非内在追求真理。

item.title: Delayed Backdoor Attacks: Exploring the Temporal Dimension as a New Attack Surface in Pre-Trained Models
item.content:encoded: Backdoor attacks against pre-trained models (PTMs) have traditionally operated under an ``immediacy assumption,'' where malicious behavior manifests instantly upon trigger occurrence. This work revisits and challenges this paradigm by introducing \textit{\textbf{Delayed Backdoor Attacks (DBA)}}, a new class of threats in which activation is temporally decoupled from trigger exposure. We propose that this \textbf{temporal dimension} is the key to unlocking a previously infeasible class of attacks: those that use common, everyday words as triggers. To examine the feasibility of this paradigm, we design and implement a proof-of-concept prototype, termed \underline{D}elayed Backdoor Attacks Based on \underline{N}onlinear \underline{D}ecay (DND). DND embeds a lightweight, stateful logic module that postpones activation until a configurable threshold is reached, producing a distinct latency phase followed by a controlled outbreak. We derive a formal model to characterize this latency behavior and propose a dual-metric evaluation framework (ASR and ASR$_{delay}$) to empirically measure the delay effect. Extensive experiments on four (natural language processing)NLP benchmarks validate the core capabilities of DND: it remains dormant for a controllable duration, sustains high clean accuracy ($\ge$94\%), and achieves near-perfect post-activation attack success rates ($\approx$99\%, The average of other methods is below 95\%.). Moreover, DND exhibits resilience against several state-of-the-art defenses. This study provides the first empirical evidence that the temporal dimension constitutes a viable yet unprotected attack surface in PTMs, underscoring the need for next-generation, stateful, and time-aware defense mechanisms.

item.title: Temporal Straightening for Latent Planning
item.content:encoded: Learning good representations is essential for latent planning with world models. While pretrained visual encoders produce strong semantic visual features, they are not tailored to planning and contain information irrelevant -- or even detrimental -- to planning. Inspired by the perceptual straightening hypothesis in human visual processing, we introduce temporal straightening to improve representation learning for latent planning. Using a curvature regularizer that encourages locally straightened latent trajectories, we jointly learn an encoder and a predictor. We show that reducing curvature this way makes the Euclidean distance in latent space a better proxy for the geodesic distance and improves the conditioning of the planning objective. We demonstrate empirically that temporal straightening makes gradient-based planning more stable and yields significantly higher success rates across a suite of goal-reaching tasks.

item.title: Manifold-Optimal Guidance: A Unified Riemannian Control View of Diffusion Guidance
item.content:encoded: Classifier-Free Guidance (CFG) serves as the de facto control mechanism for conditional diffusion, yet high guidance scales notoriously induce oversaturation, texture artifacts, and structural collapse. We attribute this failure to a geometric mismatch: standard CFG performs Euclidean extrapolation in ambient space, inadvertently driving sampling trajectories off the high-density data manifold. To resolve this, we present Manifold-Optimal Guidance (MOG), a framework that reformulates guidance as a local optimal control problem. MOG yields a closed-form, geometry-aware Riemannian update that corrects off-manifold drift without requiring retraining. Leveraging this perspective, we further introduce Auto-MOG, a dynamic energy-balancing schedule that adaptively calibrates guidance strength, effectively eliminating the need for manual hyperparameter tuning. Extensive validation demonstrates that MOG yields superior fidelity and alignment compared to baselines, with virtually no added computational overhead.

item.title: AS-Bridge: A Bidirectional Generative Framework Bridging Next-Generation Astronomical Surveys
item.content:encoded: The upcoming decade of observational cosmology will be shaped by large sky surveys, such as the ground-based LSST at the Vera C. Rubin Observatory and the space-based Euclid mission. While they promise an unprecedented view of the Universe across depth, resolution, and wavelength, their differences in observational modality, sky coverage, point-spread function, and scanning cadence make joint analysis beneficial, but also challenging. To facilitate joint analysis, we introduce A(stronomical)S(urvey)-Bridge, a bidirectional generative model that translates between ground- and space-based observations. AS-Bridge learns a diffusion model that employs a stochastic Brownian Bridge process between the LSST and Euclid observations. The two surveys have overlapping sky regions, where we can explicitly model the conditional probabilistic distribution between them. We show that this formulation enables new scientific capabilities beyond single-survey analysis, including faithful probabilistic predictions of missing survey observations and inter-survey detection of rare events. These results establish the feasibility of inter-survey generative modeling. AS-Bridge is therefore well-positioned to serve as a complementary component of future LSST-Euclid joint data pipelines, enhancing the scientific return once data from both surveys become available. Data and code are available at \href{https://github.com/ZHANG7DC/AS-Bridge}{https://github.com/ZHANG7DC/AS-Bridge}.

item.title: O3N: Omnidirectional Open-Vocabulary Occupancy Prediction
item.content:encoded: Understanding and reconstructing the 3D world through omnidirectional perception is an inevitable trend in the development of autonomous agents and embodied intelligence. However, existing 3D occupancy prediction methods are constrained by limited perspective inputs and predefined training distribution, making them difficult to apply to embodied agents that require comprehensive and safe perception of scenes in open world exploration. To address this, we present O3N, the first purely visual, end-to-end Omnidirectional Open-vocabulary Occupancy predictioN framework. O3N embeds omnidirectional voxels in a polar-spiral topology via the Polar-spiral Mamba (PsM) module, enabling continuous spatial representation and long-range context modeling across 360°. The Occupancy Cost Aggregation (OCA) module introduces a principled mechanism for unifying geometric and semantic supervision within the voxel space, ensuring consistency between the reconstructed geometry and the underlying semantic structure. Moreover, Natural Modality Alignment (NMA) establishes a gradient-free alignment pathway that harmonizes visual features, voxel embeddings, and text semantics, forming a consistent "pixel-voxel-text" representation triad. Extensive experiments on multiple models demonstrate that our method not only achieves state-of-the-art performance on QuadOcc and Human360Occ benchmarks but also exhibits remarkable cross-scene generalization and semantic scalability, paving the way toward universal 3D world modeling. The source code will be made publicly available at https://github.com/MengfeiD/O3N.

item.title: Hybrid Energy-Aware Reward Shaping: A Unified Lightweight Physics-Guided Methodology for Policy Optimization
item.content:encoded: Deep reinforcement learning excels in continuous control but often requires extensive exploration, while physics-based models demand complete equations and suffer cubic complexity. This study proposes Hybrid Energy-Aware Reward Shaping (H-EARS), unifying potential-based reward shaping with energy-aware action regularization. H-EARS constrains action magnitude while balancing task-specific and energy-based potentials via functional decomposition, achieving linear complexity O(n) by capturing dominant energy components without full dynamics. We establish a theoretical foundation including: (1) functional independence for separate task/energy optimization; (2) energy-based convergence acceleration; (3) convergence guarantees under function approximation; and (4) approximate potential error bounds. Lyapunov stability connections are analyzed as heuristic guides. Experiments across baselines show improved convergence, stability, and energy efficiency. Vehicle simulations validate applicability in safety-critical domains under extreme conditions. Results confirm that integrating lightweight physics priors enhances model-free RL without complete system models, enabling transfer from lab research to industrial applications.

item.title: Causal Prosody Mediation for Text-to-Speech:Counterfactual Training of Duration, Pitch, and Energy in FastSpeech2
item.content:encoded: We propose a novel causal prosody mediation framework for expressive text-to-speech (TTS) synthesis. Our approach augments the FastSpeech2 architecture with explicit emotion conditioning and introduces counterfactual training objectives to disentangle emotional prosody from linguistic content. By formulating a structural causal model of how text (content), emotion, and speaker jointly influence prosody (duration, pitch, energy) and ultimately the speech waveform, we derive two complementary loss terms: an Indirect Path Constraint (IPC) to enforce that emotion affects speech only through prosody, and a Counterfactual Prosody Constraint (CPC) to encourage distinct prosody patterns for different emotions. The resulting model is trained on multi-speaker emotional corpora (LibriTTS, EmoV-DB, VCTK) with a combined objective that includes standard spectrogram reconstruction and variance prediction losses alongside our causal losses. In evaluations on expressive speech synthesis, our method achieves significantly improved prosody manipulation and emotion rendering, with higher mean opinion scores (MOS) and emotion accuracy than baseline FastSpeech2 variants. We also observe better intelligibility (low WER) and speaker consistency when transferring emotions across speakers. Extensive ablations confirm that the causal objectives successfully separate prosody attribution, yielding an interpretable model that allows controlled counterfactual prosody editing (e.g. "same utterance, different emotion") without compromising naturalness. We discuss the implications for identifiability in prosody modeling and outline limitations such as the assumption that emotion effects are fully captured by pitch, duration, and energy. Our work demonstrates how integrating causal learning principles into TTS can improve controllability and expressiveness in generated speech.

item.title: An Automatic Text Classification Method Based on Hierarchical Taxonomies, Neural Networks and Document Embedding: The NETHIC Tool
item.content:encoded: This work describes an automatic text classification method implemented in a software tool called NETHIC, which takes advantage of the inner capabilities of highly-scalable neural networks combined with the expressiveness of hierarchical taxonomies. As such, NETHIC succeeds in bringing about a mechanism for text classification that proves to be significantly effective as well as efficient. The tool had undergone an experimentation process against both a generic and a domain-specific corpus, outputting promising results. On the basis of this experimentation, NETHIC has been now further refined and extended by adding a document embedding mechanism, which has shown improvements in terms of performance on the individual networks and on the whole hierarchical model.

item.title: SPEGC: Continual Test-Time Adaptation via Semantic-Prompt-Enhanced Graph Clustering for Medical Image Segmentation
item.content:encoded: In medical image segmentation tasks, the domain gap caused by the difference in data collection between training and testing data seriously hinders the deployment of pre-trained models in clinical practice. Continual Test-Time Adaptation (CTTA) aims to enable pre-trained models to adapt to continuously changing unlabeled domains, providing an effective approach to solving this problem. However, existing CTTA methods often rely on unreliable supervisory signals, igniting a self-reinforcing cycle of error accumulation that culminates in catastrophic performance degradation. To overcome these challenges, we propose a CTTA via Semantic-Prompt-Enhanced Graph Clustering (SPEGC) for medical image segmentation. First, we design a semantic prompt feature enhancement mechanism that utilizes decoupled commonality and heterogeneity prompt pools to inject global contextual information into local features, alleviating their susceptibility to noise interference under domain shift. Second, based on these enhanced features, we design a differentiable graph clustering solver. This solver reframes global edge sparsification as an optimal transport problem, allowing it to distill a raw similarity matrix into a refined and high-order structural representation in an end-to-end manner. Finally, this robust structural representation is used to guide model adaptation, ensuring predictions are consistent at a cluster-level and dynamically adjusting decision boundaries. Extensive experiments demonstrate that SPEGC outperforms other state-of-the-art CTTA methods on two medical image segmentation benchmarks. The source code is available at https://github.com/Jwei-Z/SPEGC-for-MIS.

item.title: LABSHIELD: A Multimodal Benchmark for Safety-Critical Reasoning and Planning in Scientific Laboratories
item.content:encoded: Artificial intelligence is increasingly catalyzing scientific automation, with multimodal large language model (MLLM) agents evolving from lab assistants into self-driving lab operators. This transition imposes stringent safety requirements on laboratory environments, where fragile glassware, hazardous substances, and high-precision laboratory equipment render planning errors or misinterpreted risks potentially irreversible. However, the safety awareness and decision-making reliability of embodied agents in such high-stakes settings remain insufficiently defined and evaluated. To bridge this gap, we introduce LABSHIELD, a realistic multi-view benchmark designed to assess MLLMs in hazard identification and safety-critical reasoning. Grounded in U.S. Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) standards and the Globally Harmonized System (GHS), LABSHIELD establishes a rigorous safety taxonomy spanning 164 operational tasks with diverse manipulation complexities and risk profiles. We evaluate 20 proprietary models, 9 open-source models, and 3 embodied models under a dual-track evaluation framework. Our results reveal a systematic gap between general-domain MCQ accuracy and Semi-open QA safety performance, with models exhibiting an average drop of 32.0% in professional laboratory scenarios, particularly in hazard interpretation and safety-aware planning. These findings underscore the urgent necessity for safety-centric reasoning frameworks to ensure reliable autonomous scientific experimentation in embodied laboratory contexts. The full dataset will be released soon.

item.title: WORKSWORLD: A Domain for Integrated Numeric Planning and Scheduling of Distributed Pipelined Workflows
item.content:encoded: This work pursues automated planning and scheduling of distributed data pipelines, or workflows. We develop a general workflow and resource graph representation that includes both data processing and sharing components with corresponding network interfaces for scheduling. Leveraging these graphs, we introduce WORKSWORLD, a new domain for numeric domain-independent planners designed for permanently scheduled workflows, like ingest pipelines. Our framework permits users to define data sources, available workflow components, and desired data destinations and formats without explicitly declaring the entire workflow graph as a goal. The planner solves a joint planning and scheduling problem, producing a plan that both builds the workflow graph and schedules its components on the resource graph. We empirically show that a state-of-the-art numeric planner running on commodity hardware with one hour of CPU time and 30GB of memory can solve linear-chain workflows of up to 14 components across eight sites.

item.title: Efficient Generative Modeling with Unitary Matrix Product States Using Riemannian Optimization
item.content:encoded: Tensor networks, which are originally developed for characterizing complex quantum many-body systems, have recently emerged as a powerful framework for capturing high-dimensional probability distributions with strong physical interpretability. This paper systematically studies matrix product states (MPS) for generative modeling and shows that unitary MPS, which is a tensor-network architecture that is both simple and expressive, offers clear benefits for unsupervised learning by reducing ambiguity in parameter updates and improving efficiency. To overcome the inefficiency of standard gradient-based MPS training, we develop a Riemannian optimization approach that casts probabilistic modeling as an optimization problem with manifold constraints, and further derive an efficient space-decoupling algorithm. Experiments on Bars-and-Stripes and EMNIST datasets demonstrate fast adaptation to data structure, stable updates, and strong performance while maintaining the efficiency and expressive power of MPS.

item.title: Locating Demographic Bias at the Attention-Head Level in CLIP's Vision Encoder
item.content:encoded: Standard fairness audits of foundation models quantify that a model is biased, but not where inside the network the bias resides. We propose a mechanistic fairness audit that combines projected residual-stream decomposition, zero-shot Concept Activation Vectors, and bias-augmented TextSpan analysis to locate demographic bias at the level of individual attention heads in vision transformers. As a feasibility case study, we apply this pipeline to the CLIP ViT-L-14 encoder on 42 profession classes of the FACET benchmark, auditing both gender and age bias. For gender, the pipeline identifies four terminal-layer heads whose ablation reduces global bias (Cramer's V: 0.381 -> 0.362) while marginally improving accuracy (+0.42%); a layer-matched random control confirms that this effect is specific to the identified heads. A single head in the final layer contributes to the majority of the reduction in the most stereotyped classes, and class-level analysis shows that corrected predictions shift toward the correct occupation. For age, the same pipeline identifies candidate heads, but ablation produces weaker and less consistent effects, suggesting that age bias is encoded more diffusely than gender bias in this model. These results provide preliminary evidence that head-level bias localisation is feasible for discriminative vision encoders and that the degree of localisability may vary across protected attributes. keywords: Bias . CLIP . Mechanistic Interpretability . Vision Transformer . Fairness

item.title: Multi-Agent Collaboration for Automated Design Exploration on High Performance Computing Systems
item.content:encoded: Today's scientific challenges, from climate modeling to Inertial Confinement Fusion design to novel material design, require exploring huge design spaces. In order to enable high-impact scientific discovery, we need to scale up our ability to test hypotheses, generate results, and learn from them rapidly. We present MADA (Multi-Agent Design Assistant), a Large Language Model (LLM) powered multi-agent framework that coordinates specialized agents for complex design workflows. A Job Management Agent (JMA) launches and manages ensemble simulations on HPC systems, a Geometry Agent (GA) generates meshes, and an Inverse Design Agent (IDA) proposes new designs informed by simulation outcomes. While general purpose, we focus development and validation on Richtmyer--Meshkov Instability (RMI) suppression, a critical challenge in Inertial Confinement Fusion. We evaluate on two complementary settings: running a hydrodynamics simulations on HPC systems, and using a pre-trained machine learning surrogate for rapid design exploration. Our results demonstrate that the MADA system successfully executes iterative design refinement, automatically improving designs toward optimal RMI suppression with minimal manual intervention. Our framework reduces cumbersome manual workflow setup, and enables automated design exploration at scale. More broadly, it demonstrates a reusable pattern for coupling reasoning, simulation, specialized tools, and coordinated workflows to accelerate scientific discovery.

item.title: Large Language Models for Biomedical Article Classification
item.content:encoded: This work presents a systematic and in-depth investigation of the utility of large language models as text classifiers for biomedical article classification. The study uses several small and mid-size open source models, as well as selected closed source ones, and is more comprehensive than most prior work with respect to the scope of evaluated configurations: different types of prompts, output processing methods for generating both class and class probability predictions, as well as few-shot example counts and selection methods. The performance of the most successful configurations is compared to that of conventional classification algorithms. The obtained average PR AUC over 15 challenging datasets above 0.4 for zero-shot prompting and nearly 0.5 for few-shot prompting comes close to that of the naïve Bayes classifier (0.5), the random forest algorithm (0.5 with default settings or 0.55 with hyperparameter tuning) and fine-tuned transformer models (0.5). These results confirm the utility of large language models as text classifiers for non-trivial domains and provide practical recommendations of the most promising setups, including in particular using output token probabilities for class probability prediction.

item.title: EVATok: Adaptive Length Video Tokenization for Efficient Visual Autoregressive Generation
item.content:encoded: Autoregressive (AR) video generative models rely on video tokenizers that compress pixels into discrete token sequences. The length of these token sequences is crucial for balancing reconstruction quality against downstream generation computational cost. Traditional video tokenizers apply a uniform token assignment across temporal blocks of different videos, often wasting tokens on simple, static, or repetitive segments while underserving dynamic or complex ones. To address this inefficiency, we introduce $\textbf{EVATok}$, a framework to produce $\textbf{E}$fficient $\textbf{V}$ideo $\textbf{A}$daptive $\textbf{Tok}$enizers. Our framework estimates optimal token assignments for each video to achieve the best quality-cost trade-off, develops lightweight routers for fast prediction of these optimal assignments, and trains adaptive tokenizers that encode videos based on the assignments predicted by routers. We demonstrate that EVATok delivers substantial improvements in efficiency and overall quality for video reconstruction and downstream AR generation. Enhanced by our advanced training recipe that integrates video semantic encoders, EVATok achieves superior reconstruction and state-of-the-art class-to-video generation on UCF-101, with at least 24.4% savings in average token usage compared to the prior state-of-the-art LARP and our fixed-length baseline.

item.title: Deep Learning Network-Temporal Models For Traffic Prediction
item.content:encoded: Time series analysis is critical for emerging net- work intelligent control and management functions. However, existing statistical-based and shallow machine learning models have shown limited prediction capabilities on multivariate time series. The intricate topological interdependency and complex temporal patterns in network data demand new model approaches. In this paper, based on a systematic multivariate time series model study, we present two deep learning models aiming for learning both temporal patterns and network topological correlations at the same time: a customized network-temporal graph attention network (GAT) model and a fine-tuned multi-modal large language model (LLM) with a clustering overture. Both models are studied against an LSTM model that already outperforms the statistical methods. Through extensive training and performance studies on a real-world network dataset, the LLM-based model demonstrates superior overall prediction and generalization performance, while the GAT model shows its strength in reducing prediction variance across the time series and horizons. More detailed analysis also reveals important insights into correlation variability and prediction distribution discrepancies over time series and different prediction horizons.

item.title: AstroSplat: Physics-Based Gaussian Splatting for Rendering and Reconstruction of Small Celestial Bodies
item.content:encoded: Image-based surface reconstruction and characterization are crucial for missions to small celestial bodies (e.g., asteroids), as it informs mission planning, navigation, and scientific analysis. Recent advances in Gaussian splatting enable high-fidelity neural scene representations but typically rely on a spherical harmonic intensity parameterization that is strictly appearance-based and does not explicitly model material properties or light-surface interactions. We introduce AstroSplat, a physics-based Gaussian splatting framework that integrates planetary reflectance models to improve the autonomous reconstruction and photometric characterization of small-body surfaces from in-situ imagery. The proposed framework is validated on real imagery taken by NASA's Dawn mission, where we demonstrate superior rendering performance and surface reconstruction accuracy compared to the typical spherical harmonic parameterization.

item.title: Deactivating Refusal Triggers: Understanding and Mitigating Overrefusal in Safety Alignment
item.content:encoded: Safety alignment aims to ensure that large language models (LLMs) refuse harmful requests by post-training on harmful queries paired with refusal answers. Although safety alignment is widely adopted in industry, the overrefusal problem where aligned LLMs also reject benign queries after safety alignment post-training, remains insufficiently studied. Such an issue degrades the usability of safety alignment in real-world applications. In this paper, we examine how overrefusal arises under safety alignment, and propose a mitigation strategy inspired by our findings. We define refusal triggers as linguistic cues in the training data that elicit refusal responses, safety alignment encourages LLMs to associate refusal triggers within a training sample with refusal responses, leading aligned LLMs to refuse harmful queries. However, the refusal triggers include not only harmful linguistic cues but also non-harmful cues, therefore causing overrefusal to benign queries. Building on this mechanistic analysis, we propose a method that explicitly considers refusal triggers in the safety alignment fine-tuning. Empirical results demonstrate that our approach achieves a more favorable trade-off between defense against jailbreak attacks and responsiveness to benign queries, outperforming prior methods. Warning: this paper contains harmful and biased sentences.

item.title: OSM-based Domain Adaptation for Remote Sensing VLMs
item.content:encoded: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) adapted to remote sensing rely heavily on domain-specific image-text supervision, yet high-quality annotations for satellite and aerial imagery remain scarce and expensive to produce. Prevailing pseudo-labeling pipelines address this gap by distilling knowledge from large frontier models, but this dependence on large teachers is costly, limits scalability, and caps achievable performance at the ceiling of the teacher. We propose OSMDA: a self-contained domain adaptation framework that eliminates this dependency. Our key insight is that a capable base VLM can serve as its own annotation engine: by pairing aerial images with rendered OpenStreetMap (OSM) tiles, we leverage optical character recognition and chart comprehension capabilities of the model to generate captions enriched by OSM's vast auxiliary metadata. The model is then fine-tuned on the resulting corpus with satellite imagery alone, yielding OSMDA-VLM, a domain-adapted VLM that requires no manual labeling and no stronger external model. We conduct exhaustive evaluations spanning 10 benchmarks across image-text-to-text tasks and comparing against 9 competitive baselines. When equally mixed with real data, our method achieves state-of-the-art results, while being substantially cheaper to train than teacher-dependent alternatives. These results suggest that, given a strong foundation model, alignment with crowd-sourced geographic data is a practical and scalable path towards remote sensing domain adaptation. Dataset and model weights will be made publicly available.

item.title: 基于时空解耦的自监督视频去噪方法F2R
item.content:encoded:
F2R通过时空解耦提升视频去噪效果。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为Frames2Residual（F2R）的自监督视频去噪框架，该框架通过将训练过程分为两个阶段：盲时序一致性建模和非盲空间纹理恢复，以解决现有方法在时空关联性上的不足。第一阶段通过帧级盲策略学习帧间一致性，生成时序一致的锚点；第二阶段利用该锚点安全地重新引入中心帧，恢复高频空间残差并保持时序稳定性。实验表明，F2R在sRGB和原始视频基准测试中均优于现有方法。

item.title: G-STAR：一种端到端的长时多说话人语音识别系统
item.content:encoded:
G-STAR实现多说话人语音的精确时间标注与身份一致性。



摘要： 本文提出G-STAR，一种用于长时多说话人语音的端到端系统，结合时间感知的说话人追踪模块和语音大模型。该系统通过结构化说话人提示和时间定位，提升语音转录的准确性，并支持组件优化和联合训练，适应不同监督条件和领域变化。实验分析了提示融合、局部与长上下文权衡以及分层目标。

item.title: GLM-OCR：高效多模态文档理解模型
item.content:encoded:
GLM-OCR是高效的0.9B参数多模态文档理解模型。



摘要： GLM-OCR是一款0.9B参数的紧凑多模态模型，用于现实世界中的文档理解。它结合了0.4B参数的CogViT视觉编码器和0.5B参数的GLM语言解码器，在计算效率和识别性能之间取得良好平衡。为提升OCR任务的解码效率，GLM-OCR引入了多标记预测机制（MTP），每步预测多个标记，提高解码吞吐量并保持低内存开销。系统采用两阶段流程：先进行布局分析，再进行区域级并行识别。在公共基准和工业场景中评估显示，GLM-OCR在文档解析、文本和公式转录、表格结构恢复和关键信息提取方面表现出色，适合资源受限的边缘部署和大规模生产系统。

item.title: 基于知识图谱的药物处方审核系统PharmGraph-Auditor
item.content:encoded:
PharmGraph-Auditor提升处方审核安全性与效率。



摘要： 本文提出PharmGraph-Auditor，一种基于可信混合药物知识库（HPKB）的处方审核系统。该系统采用虚拟知识图谱架构，结合关系型与图结构以增强推理能力。通过迭代模式优化算法构建HPKB，并引入基于知识库的验证链（CoV），将大语言模型转化为透明推理引擎，提升处方审核的准确性与可追溯性。实验表明该系统具有强大的知识提取能力，有助于药师实现更安全、高效的处方审核。

item.title: LLM评估的共识幻觉与动态评分框架研究
item.content:encoded:
LLM评估共识可能不真实，动态评分框架提升评估质量。



摘要： 本文挑战了LLM作为评估者的传统假设，指出高评估者一致性可能是虚假的。通过大规模实验，发现模型层面的高一致性掩盖了样本层面的脆弱性，并揭示了高质量输出反而获得最不一致评价的现象。其次，研究提出MERG框架，通过结合领域知识生成评分标准，显著提升了评估的一致性和意义，尤其在教育和学术领域效果显著。该成果对RLAIF中的奖励建模具有重要启示。

item.title: 基于高斯混合过程的多模态条件密度估计方法
item.content:encoded:
提出GGMP方法解决多模态条件密度估计问题。



摘要： 文章介绍了通用高斯混合过程（GGMP），这是一种基于高斯过程（GP）的方法，用于在每个输入可能对应复杂输出分布而非单一标量响应的情况下进行多模态条件密度估计。GGMP结合了局部高斯混合拟合、跨输入成分对齐和每成分异方差GP训练，生成闭式高斯混合预测密度。该方法具有可计算性，兼容标准GP求解器和可扩展方法，避免了传统多模态GP方法中指数级增长的潜在分配结构。实验表明，GGMP在具有明显非高斯性和多模态性的合成和真实数据集上表现更优。

item.title: WalkGPT：基于像素的行人导航引导模型
item.content:encoded:
WalkGPT解决复杂城市场景下的可访问性导航问题。



摘要： 本文提出WalkGPT，一种结合语言推理与分割的像素接地大型视觉语言模型，用于生成深度感知的可访问性导航指导。该模型通过多尺度查询投影器和校准文本投影器，实现细粒度的语义与空间 grounding，无需用户提供的提示或锚点。WalkGPT在包含41k行人视角图像的PAVE基准上进行了测试，表现出强大的语义推理和分割性能。

item.title: 基于人类推理的语音深度伪造检测框架
item.content:encoded:
提出HIR-SDD框架提升语音深度伪造检测效果与可解释性。



摘要： 本文提出一种新型语音深度伪造检测框架HIR-SDD，结合大音频语言模型与人类标注数据集中的链式推理方法。该方法有效提升了对新音频领域和生成器的泛化能力，并增强了预测的可解释性，能够提供符合人类认知的判断依据。实验结果表明，该方法在检测效果和解释性方面均表现出色。

item.title: 轻量级Transformer点云架构的高效训练与评估
item.content:encoded:
轻量模型在点云任务中表现优于大型模型。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于Transformer的轻量级点云架构，仅使用3.9万组点云数据进行训练，却在性能上超越了依赖更多数据的大型基础模型。实验表明，该模型接近于使用百万级多模态数据训练的先进模型，验证了精心设计的训练方案和架构的有效性。研究还进行了全面的复现实验，统一评估标准并比较多种点云架构，证明简单结构也能取得优异效果。相关代码和预训练模型已开源。

item.title: 人类提升研究在前沿AI系统决策中的挑战与应用
item.content:encoded:
人类提升研究在高风险决策中面临方法论挑战。



摘要： 文章探讨了人类提升研究（测量AI对人类表现的影响）在前沿AI系统部署和治理中的应用。尽管该方法基于成熟的因果推断框架，但其与快速演进的AI系统、变化的基准线及用户能力等特性之间的互动仍缺乏深入研究。通过对16位专家的访谈，文章揭示了标准因果假设与实际研究对象之间的张力，并分析了在生物安全、网络安全、教育和劳动力等领域中，这些挑战如何影响研究结果的内部、外部和结构有效性。文章还总结了从业者提出的解决方案，明确了人类提升研究证据在高风险决策中的局限性和适用范围。

item.title: 基于历史共识训练的变分自编码器方法防止后验崩溃
item.content:encoded:
通过GMM聚类约束防止VAE后验崩溃，无需额外稳定性条件。



摘要： 本文提出一种新的方法来解决变分自编码器（VAEs）中的后验崩溃问题。传统方法依赖于架构限制或超参数调整，而本文通过利用高斯混合模型（GMM）的多重聚类特性，引入了历史共识训练（Historical Consensus Training）。该方法通过迭代优化和选择过程逐步改进候选GMM先验，使模型在多个聚类约束下训练，从而形成一个稳定区域，排除后验崩溃的可能性。实验表明，无论解码器方差或正则化强度如何，该方法都能获得非崩溃的表示。此方法不依赖显式的稳定性条件，适用于任意神经网络架构。

item.title: 面向可及性与包容性的AI驱动数字人建模框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种平台无关的AI驱动DHM框架，支持多模态交互研究。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种平台无关的数字人建模（DHM）框架，旨在促进AI驱动的多模态交互研究。该框架通过分离感知、交互建模和推理准备，提升可重复性、可扩展性和伦理合规性。它整合了OpenBCI Galea脑电采集设备，提供同步的EEG、EMG、EOG、PPG和惯性数据，并结合SuperTux游戏环境进行交互建模。生理信号以结构化时间对齐的可观测量形式表示，便于后续AI方法应用。框架支持计算任务原语和时间戳事件标记，确保跨异构传感器和平台的一致性。技术验证确认了数据完整性与同步性，未涉及人类受试者评估或AI推理。文章还讨论了可扩展性问题，并展示了其在无障碍交互设计和自适应系统研究中的应用潜力。

item.title: 提升网络入侵检测系统对抗攻击鲁棒性的方法研究
item.content:encoded:
本文提出一种多层防御机制以增强NIDS对对抗样本的抵抗力。



摘要： 本文研究了对抗样本对基于机器学习的网络入侵检测系统（NIDS）的威胁，并提出一种多层防御机制来提高其鲁棒性。通过生成对抗网络（GAN）和快速梯度符号法（FGSM）生成恶意流量，评估了该防御机制的有效性。该方案包含一个堆叠分类器层和一个基于自编码器的第二层，用于验证分类结果的准确性。此外，还引入了对抗训练以进一步提升系统安全性。实验在UNSW-NB15和NSL-KDD数据集上进行，结果表明该方法显著增强了系统对对抗攻击的抵抗能力。

item.title: 基于多智能体强化学习的无人机医疗配送协调框架
item.content:encoded:
本文提出一种MARL框架用于无人机医疗配送协调。



摘要： 本文研究了在不确定环境下，利用多智能体强化学习（MARL）协调无人机舰队进行医疗物资配送的问题。该框架将问题建模为部分可观测马尔可夫决策过程（POMDP），并采用近端策略优化（PPO）作为主要算法。通过真实地理数据验证，结果表明经典PPO在协调性能上优于异步和顺序学习策略，展示了强化学习在医疗物流中的应用潜力。

item.title: GeoFM在遥感影像嵌入设计中的系统分析
item.content:encoded:
研究GeoFM嵌入设计对遥感任务性能的影响。



摘要： 本文系统分析了基于地理基础模型（GeoFM）的遥感影像嵌入设计，探讨了主干架构、预训练策略、表示深度、空间聚合和表示组合对任务性能的影响。通过NeuCo-Bench实验平台，研究发现使用Transformer主干和均值池化可提供强大的默认嵌入，中间ResNet层可能优于最终层，自监督目标在特定任务中表现优异，结合不同目标的嵌入可提高鲁棒性。研究还展示了嵌入在保持任务性能的同时，可以显著压缩数据规模。

item.title: SearchLLM：面向开放性生成搜索的大型语言模型
item.content:encoded:
SearchLLM提升生成搜索质量与用户参与度。



摘要： 文章介绍了SearchLLM，这是首个面向开放性生成搜索的大型语言模型。该模型通过分层多维奖励系统，将基本约束与行为优化目标分离，以提升答案的准确性和用户满意度。其奖励模型结合规则检查和人工校准的LLM判断，生成可解释的评分向量。通过Gated Aggregation Strategy和GRPO方法进行训练，并在RedNote的AI搜索入口部署。实验结果显示，SearchLLM提升了生成质量，用户参与度提高，同时保持了严格的安全性和可靠性。

item.title: 自然历史博物馆数字化数据的自然语言交互系统设计
item.content:encoded:
通过对话式AI实现对百万级标本数据的高效查询与探索。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种基于对话式人工智能的系统设计，用于查询澳大利亚博物馆近170万份数字化标本记录。该系统结合了交互式地图和自然语言对话代理，使用户能够通过自然语言进行科学数据的探索与获取。系统利用大型语言模型的功能调用能力，从外部API动态检索结构化数据，实现实时、高效的数据库交互。该研究为自然历史博物馆的大规模数据开放提供了新思路，并为未来科学AI代理的设计提供了参考。

item.title: 神经网络训练中最佳学习率调度形状的探索
item.content:encoded:
研究发现最佳学习率调度需包含预热和衰减，常见调度非最优。



摘要： 本文探讨了神经网络训练中最佳学习率调度形状的问题。研究指出，学习率调度对训练成功至关重要，但目前缺乏统一标准。作者设计了一种搜索方法，在不同调度家族中寻找最佳形状，并发现预热和衰减是良好调度的稳健特征。实验表明，常用调度家族在所选任务中并非最优，且权重衰减等其他超参数也会影响最优调度形状。这是目前关于深度神经网络训练近似最优调度形状最全面的研究。

item.title: 基于最近邻距离比的内在维度估计方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新的内在维度估计方法，具有通用性且效果优异。



摘要： 本文研究了数据内在维度估计问题，该问题在机器学习和计算机视觉中具有重要意义。现有方法通常依赖几何或分布假设，当这些假设不成立时性能会显著下降。为此，作者提出了一种基于最近邻距离比的新估计方法，计算简单且效果优于现有技术。更重要的是，理论分析证明该方法具有通用性，即无论数据分布如何都能收敛到真实内在维度。实验结果表明，该方法在基准流形和真实数据集上表现良好。

item.title: 基于深度学习的参数估计模型研究
item.content:encoded:
深度学习用于参数估计，提升模型准确性与鲁棒性。



摘要： 本文探讨了利用深度学习模型分析参数统计模型的方法，重点研究了基于模拟框架的贝叶斯和频率模型。通过使用总结网络和归一化流，模型能够有效逼近后验分布并解决逆问题。研究提出了一种分枝网络结构，通过降维层和全连接层实现参数估计，并在模拟中验证了其在有限样本、数据污染和算法近似方面的优势。该方法在遗传数据的EM算法中表现出色，展示了模拟仅方法在复杂建模任务中的潜力。

item.title: 基于LLM-as-a-Judge的文本评估框架与多模态聚类方法
item.content:encoded:
提出MultiwayPAM方法优化LLM评估文本质量的效率与公平性。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种名为LLM-as-a-Judge的灵活文本评估框架，该框架通过调整提示模板，可以从不同角度对文本质量进行评分。文章指出，使用该框架面临两大挑战：大规模文本评估时的计算成本较高，以及LLM评估者的内在偏差。为解决这些问题，作者提出了一种新的张量聚类方法MultiwayPAM，能够同时估计数据张量中各模式的聚类成员和中心点。通过分析MultiwayPAM得到的中心点，可以深入了解各个问题、回答者和评估者的聚类归属。实验表明，该方法在两个实际数据集上具有良好的效果。

item.title: 基于领域自适应的健康指标建模方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新框架提升健康指标建模性能。



摘要： 本文针对健康指标建模中因工况变化导致的分布不匹配问题，提出了一种结合退化阶段同步采样和跨域对齐融合大自编码器的领域自适应框架。该框架通过检测退化阶段的核变化点，实现源域与目标域样本在故障阶段上的同步采样，有效缓解了因阶段不对齐导致的损失误导。同时，利用大卷积核和跨注意力机制增强时间特征提取能力，提升了模型的域不变表示学习效果。实验结果表明，该方法在两个轴承数据集上相比现有方法平均提升24.1%。

item.title: 基于Med-DualLoRA的联邦微调在3D心脏磁共振疾病检测中的应用
item.content:encoded:
提出Med-DualLoRA框架提升3D CMR疾病检测性能与效率。



摘要： 本文研究了在多中心3D心脏磁共振（CMR）疾病检测任务中，使用联邦微调方法对基础模型进行优化。针对传统方法在异构数据下的性能瓶颈和通信开销问题，作者提出Med-DualLoRA框架，通过解耦全局共享和局部低秩适配模块，实现更高效的模型个性化与隐私保护。实验表明，该方法在ACDC和M&Ms数据集上取得了显著的性能提升，同时保持了良好的通信效率，为医疗基础模型的分布式适应提供了可行方案。

item.title: 多语言推理训练场：跨语言推理问题生成与研究支持
item.content:encoded:
多语言推理训练场生成跨语言推理问题，支持模型研究。



摘要： 本文介绍了多语言推理训练场（Multilingual Reasoning Gym），这是对原有Reasoning Gym的扩展，能够在14种语言中程序化生成可验证的推理问题。通过在10种语言中进行母语者验证并进行代码或模板适配，确保了语言自然性。该平台保留了原Reasoning Gym的核心优势，如无限问题生成和难度可调，并适用于基于可验证奖励的强化学习和评估设置。由于其程序化特性，该训练场支持大规模跨语言数据生成，有助于多语言推理模型的研究。

item.title: 基于语义地标的农业环境精准定位方法研究
item.content:encoded:
SLPF提升葡萄园中重复环境下的定位精度。



摘要： 本文针对葡萄园中因行间结构相似导致的定位误差问题，提出一种结合语义地标的粒子滤波定位方法（SLPF）。该方法利用LiDAR检测树干和杆件，并将其转化为语义墙以增强相邻行之间的区分度。同时引入GNSS作为轻量先验信息，在语义观测稀疏时保持定位稳定。实验表明，SLPF在10行葡萄园中相比几何定位、视觉定位和GNSS基线分别提升了22%-65%的定位精度，显著提高了行间定位正确率和横向误差控制。

item.title: 提升计算机图形质量评估的视觉语言模型方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出新框架提升VLM对CG质量的评估能力。



摘要： 本文针对现有计算机图形质量评估方法的不足，提出从用户视角识别六种关键感知维度，并构建包含3500张CG图像及其质量描述的数据集。通过构建问答基准测试现有视觉语言模型（VLMs）的表现，发现其在细粒度CG质量判断上存在不足。研究进一步提出基于检索增强生成的双流框架，有效提升VLMs在CG质量评估任务中的性能。

item.title: 基于在线强化学习的图生成模型优化方法Graph-GRPO
item.content:encoded:
Graph-GRPO提升图生成模型性能，实现高效采样与质量优化。



摘要： 本文提出Graph-GRPO，一种用于训练图生成模型（GFM）的在线强化学习框架。该方法通过推导GFM的转移概率表达式，替代蒙特卡洛采样，实现端到端的RL训练。同时引入局部优化策略，通过随机扰动节点和边来提升生成质量。实验表明，Graph-GRPO在合成和真实数据集上表现优异，在仅50步去噪的情况下，分别获得95.0%和97.5%的有效性、唯一性和新颖性评分，并在分子优化任务中超越现有方法。

item.title: 深度神经网络的不确定性量化方法比较研究
item.content:encoded:
比较蒙特卡洛Dropout与置信预测在深度学习中的不确定性评估效果。



摘要： 本文对比了两种常用的不确定性量化方法：基于蒙特卡洛Dropout的贝叶斯近似和非参数置信预测框架。通过在Fashion-MNIST数据集上对H-CNN VGG16和GoogLeNet进行实验，发现虽然H-CNN VGG16具有更高的预测准确率，但其表现出明显的过度自信；而GoogLeNet则提供了更可靠的不确定性估计。此外，置信预测方法在统计上具有保证的预测集，显示出在高风险决策场景中的实际价值。研究强调了在评估模型性能时应超越准确率，以提升深度学习系统的可靠性与可信度。

item.title: 基于VERI-DPO的临床摘要生成方法改进
item.content:encoded:
VERI-DPO提升临床摘要准确性与可靠性。



摘要： 本文提出VERI-DPO方法，通过声明验证和直接偏好优化提升临床摘要质量。研究使用MIMIC-III-Ext-VeriFact-BHC数据集，训练检索增强验证器以标记声明与证据的关系。该方法通过聚合句级声明得分，生成长度可控、矛盾锚定的偏好对，有效减少未支持声明比例，提高摘要有效性。

item.title: 基于数据驱动的MHD状态重建方法研究
item.content:encoded:
SHRED网络实现MHD状态高效重建。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于数据驱动的MHD状态重建框架，结合奇异值分解（SVD）与SHRED神经网络，从有限传感器测量数据中恢复全时空状态。该方法应用于压缩铅锂流在阶梯通道中的MHD测试案例，仅需三个温度传感器输入即可准确重建速度、压力和温度场。通过30种随机传感器配置验证了其鲁棒性，结果表明SHRED在训练集外磁场强度下仍能保持高精度，为核聚变多物理场问题提供了高效的替代建模策略，适用于实时监测与控制。
