item.title: LLandMark: A Multi-Agent Framework for Landmark-Aware Multimodal Interactive Video Retrieval
item.content:encoded: The increasing diversity and scale of video data demand retrieval systems capable of multimodal understanding, adaptive reasoning, and domain-specific knowledge integration. This paper presents LLandMark, a modular multi-agent framework for landmark-aware multimodal video retrieval to handle real-world complex queries. The framework features specialized agents that collaborate across four stages: query parsing and planning, landmark reasoning, multimodal retrieval, and reranked answer synthesis. A key component, the Landmark Knowledge Agent, detects cultural or spatial landmarks and reformulates them into descriptive visual prompts, enhancing CLIP-based semantic matching for Vietnamese scenes. To expand capabilities, we introduce an LLM-assisted image-to-image pipeline, where a large language model (Gemini 2.5 Flash) autonomously detects landmarks, generates image search queries, retrieves representative images, and performs CLIP-based visual similarity matching, removing the need for manual image input. In addition, an OCR refinement module leveraging Gemini and LlamaIndex improves Vietnamese text recognition. Experimental results show that LLandMark achieves adaptive, culturally grounded, and explainable retrieval performance.

item.title: Utonia: Toward One Encoder for All Point Clouds
item.content:encoded: We dream of a future where point clouds from all domains can come together to shape a single model that benefits them all. Toward this goal, we present Utonia, a first step toward training a single self-supervised point transformer encoder across diverse domains, spanning remote sensing, outdoor LiDAR, indoor RGB-D sequences, object-centric CAD models, and point clouds lifted from RGB-only videos. Despite their distinct sensing geometries, densities, and priors, Utonia learns a consistent representation space that transfers across domains. This unification improves perception capability while revealing intriguing emergent behaviors that arise only when domains are trained jointly. Beyond perception, we observe that Utonia representations can also benefit embodied and multimodal reasoning: conditioning vision-language-action policies on Utonia features improves robotic manipulation, and integrating them into vision-language models yields gains on spatial reasoning. We hope Utonia can serve as a step toward foundation models for sparse 3D data, and support downstream applications in AR/VR, robotics, and autonomous driving.

item.title: CAPT: Confusion-Aware Prompt Tuning for Reducing Vision-Language Misalignment
item.content:encoded: Vision-language models like CLIP have achieved remarkable progress in cross-modal representation learning, yet suffer from systematic misclassifications among visually and semantically similar categories. We observe that such confusion patterns are not random but persistently occur between specific category pairs, revealing the model's intrinsic bias and limited fine-grained discriminative ability. To address this, we propose CAPT, a Confusion-Aware Prompt Tuning framework that enables models to learn from their own misalignment. Specifically, we construct a Confusion Bank to explicitly model stable confusion relationships across categories and misclassified samples. On this basis, we introduce a Semantic Confusion Miner (SEM) to capture global inter-class confusion through semantic difference and commonality prompts, and a Sample Confusion Miner (SAM) to retrieve representative misclassified instances from the bank and capture sample-level cues through a Diff-Manner Adapter that integrates global and local contexts. To further unify confusion information across different granularities, a Multi-Granularity Difference Expert (MGDE) module is designed to jointly leverage semantic- and sample-level experts for more robust confusion-aware reasoning. Extensive experiments on 11 benchmark datasets demonstrate that our method significantly reduces confusion-induced errors while enhancing the discriminability and generalization of both base and novel classes, successfully resolving 50.72 percent of confusable sample pairs. Code will be released at https://github.com/greatest-gourmet/CAPT.

item.title: ACE-Merging: Data-Free Model Merging with Adaptive Covariance Estimation
item.content:encoded: Model merging aims to combine multiple task-specific expert models into a single model while preserving generalization across diverse tasks. However, interference among experts, especially when they are trained on different objectives, often leads to significant performance degradation. Despite recent progress, resolving this interference without data access, retraining, or architectural modification remains a fundamental challenge. This paper provides a theoretical analysis demonstrating that the input covariance of each task, which is a key factor for optimal merging, can be implicitly estimated from the parameter differences of its fine-tuned model, even in a fully data-free setting. Building on this insight, we introduce \acem, an Adaptive Covariance Estimation framework that effectively mitigates inter-task interference. Our approach features a principled, closed-form solution that contrasts with prior iterative or heuristic methods. Extensive experiments on both vision and language benchmarks demonstrate that \acem sets a new state-of-the-art among data-free methods. It consistently outperforms existing baselines; for example, \acem achieves an average absolute improvement of 4\% over the previous methods across seven tasks on GPT-2. Owing to its efficient closed-form formulation, \acem delivers superior performance with a modest computational cost, providing a practical and theoretically grounded solution for model merging.

item.title: Think-as-You-See: Streaming Chain-of-Thought Reasoning for Large Vision-Language Models
item.content:encoded: Large Vision Language Models (LVLMs) exhibit strong Chain-of-Thought (CoT) capabilities, yet most existing paradigms assume full-video availability before inference, a batch-style process misaligned with real-world video streams where information arrives sequentially. Motivated by the streaming nature of video data, we investigate two streaming reasoning paradigms for LVLMs. The first, an interleaved paradigm, alternates between receiving frames and producing partial reasoning but remains constrained by strictly ordered cache updates. To better match streaming inputs, we propose \textbf{Think-as-You-See (TaYS)}, a unified framework enabling true concurrent reasoning. TaYS integrates parallelized CoT generation, stream-constrained training, and stream-parallel inference. It further employs temporally aligned reasoning units, streaming attention masks and positional encodings, and a dual KV-cache that decouples visual encoding from textual reasoning. We evaluate all paradigms on the Qwen2.5-VL family across representative video CoT tasks, including event dynamics analysis, causal reasoning, and thematic understanding. Experiments show that TaYS consistently outperforms both batch and interleaved baselines, improving reasoning performance while substantially reducing time-to-first-token (TTFT) and overall reasoning delay. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of data-aligned streaming reasoning in enabling efficient and responsive video understanding for LVLMs. We release our code at \href{https://github.com/EIT-NLP/StreamingLLM/tree/main/TaYS}{this repository.}

item.title: TenExp: Mixture-of-Experts-Based Tensor Decomposition Structure Search Framework
item.content:encoded: Recently, tensor decompositions continue to emerge and receive increasing attention. Selecting a suitable tensor decomposition to exactly capture the low-rank structures behind the data is at the heart of the tensor decomposition field, which remains a challenging and relatively under-explored problem. Current tensor decomposition structure search methods are still confined by a fixed factor-interaction family (e.g., tensor contraction) and cannot deliver the mixture of decompositions. To address this problem, we elaborately design a mixture-of-experts-based tensor decomposition structure search framework (termed as TenExp), which allows us to dynamically select and activate suitable tensor decompositions in an unsupervised fashion. This framework enjoys two unique advantages over the state-of-the-art tensor decomposition structure search methods. Firstly, TenExp can provide a suitable single decomposition beyond a fixed factor-interaction family. Secondly, TenExp can deliver a suitable mixture of decompositions beyond a single decomposition. Theoretically, we also provide the approximation error bound of TenExp, which reveals the approximation capability of TenExp. Extensive experiments on both synthetic and realistic datasets demonstrate the superiority of the proposed TenExp compared to the state-of-the-art tensor decomposition-based methods.

item.title: MaBERT:A Padding Safe Interleaved Transformer Mamba Hybrid Encoder for Efficient Extended Context Masked Language Modeling
item.content:encoded: Self attention encoders such as Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers(BERT) scale quadratically with sequence length, making long context modeling expensive. Linear time state space models, such as Mamba, are efficient; however, they show limitations in modeling global interactions and can suffer from padding induced state contamination. We propose MaBERT, a hybrid encoder that interleaves Transformer layers for global dependency modeling with Mamba layers for linear time state updates. This design alternates global contextual integration with fast state accumulation, enabling efficient training and inference on long inputs. To stabilize variable length batching, we introduce paddingsafe masking, which blocks state propagation through padded positions, and mask aware attention pooling, which aggregates information only from valid tokens. On GLUE, MaBERT achieves the best mean score on five of the eight tasks, with strong performance on the CoLA and sentence pair inference tasks. When extending the context from 512 to 4,096 tokens, MaBERT reduces training time and inference latency by 2.36x and 2.43x, respectively, relative to the average of encoder baselines, demonstrating a practical long context efficient encoder.

item.title: ACE-Brain-0: Spatial Intelligence as a Shared Scaffold for Universal Embodiments
item.content:encoded: Universal embodied intelligence demands robust generalization across heterogeneous embodiments, such as autonomous driving, robotics, and unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs). However, existing embodied brain in training a unified model over diverse embodiments frequently triggers long-tail data, gradient interference, and catastrophic forgetting, making it notoriously difficult to balance universal generalization with domain-specific proficiency. In this report, we introduce ACE-Brain-0, a generalist foundation brain that unifies spatial reasoning, autonomous driving, and embodied manipulation within a single multimodal large language model~(MLLM). Our key insight is that spatial intelligence serves as a universal scaffold across diverse physical embodiments: although vehicles, robots, and UAVs differ drastically in morphology, they share a common need for modeling 3D mental space, making spatial cognition a natural, domain-agnostic foundation for cross-embodiment transfer. Building on this insight, we propose the Scaffold-Specialize-Reconcile~(SSR) paradigm, which first establishes a shared spatial foundation, then cultivates domain-specialized experts, and finally harmonizes them through data-free model merging. Furthermore, we adopt Group Relative Policy Optimization~(GRPO) to strengthen the model's comprehensive capability. Extensive experiments demonstrate that ACE-Brain-0 achieves competitive and even state-of-the-art performance across 24 spatial and embodiment-related benchmarks.

item.title: Infinite dimensional generative sensing
item.content:encoded: Deep generative models have become a standard for modeling priors for inverse problems, going beyond classical sparsity-based methods. However, existing theoretical guarantees are mostly confined to finite-dimensional vector spaces, creating a gap when the physical signals are modeled as functions in Hilbert spaces. This work presents a rigorous framework for generative compressed sensing in Hilbert spaces. We extend the notion of local coherence in an infinite-dimensional setting, to derive optimal, resolution-independent sampling distributions. Thanks to a generalization of the Restricted Isometry Property, we show that stable recovery holds when the number of measurements is proportional to the prior's intrinsic dimension (up to logarithmic factors), independent of the ambient dimension. Finally, numerical experiments on the Darcy flow equation validate our theoretical findings and demonstrate that in severely undersampled regimes, employing lower-resolution generators acts as an implicit regularizer, improving reconstruction stability.

item.title: Stabilized Adaptive Loss and Residual-Based Collocation for Physics-Informed Neural Networks
item.content:encoded: Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) have been recognized as a mesh-free alternative to solve partial differential equations where physics information is incorporated. However, in dealing with problems characterized by high stiffness or shock-dominated dynamics, traditional PINNs have been found to have limitations, including unbalanced training and inaccuracy in solution, even with small physics residuals. In this research, we seek to address these limitations using the viscous Burgers' equation with low viscosity and the Allen-Cahn equation as test problems. In addressing unbalanced training, we have developed a new adaptive loss balancing scheme using smoothed gradient norms to ensure satisfaction of initial and boundary conditions. Further, to address inaccuracy in the solution, we have developed an adaptive residual-based collocation scheme to improve the accuracy of solutions in the regions with high physics residuals. The proposed new approach significantly improves solution accuracy with consistent satisfaction of physics residuals. For instance, in the case of Burgers' equation, the relative L2 error is reduced by about 44 percent compared to traditional PINNs, while for the Allen-Cahn equation, the relative L2 error is reduced by approximately 70 percent. Additionally, we show the trustworthy solution comparison of the proposed method using a robust finite difference solver.

item.title: 基于SAE的模型迁移性评分方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新的模型迁移性评分方法，用于预测预训练模型的微调效果。



摘要： 本文研究了预训练大语言模型在下游任务中的迁移性能问题。通过引入基于稀疏自编码器（SAE）的迁移性评分（STS），该方法能够提前预测模型在微调后的表现。实验表明，STS与实际性能变化具有高度相关性，相关系数超过0.7。此外，研究还初步探索了STS在强化学习中的应用潜力，认为其可作为指导后训练策略的可解释工具。

item.title: EIMC：一种高效的多模态协同感知方法
item.content:encoded:
EIMC提升自动驾驶安全，减少通信带宽消耗。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为EIMC的新型多模态协同感知方法，旨在提升自动驾驶的安全性。与传统方法不同，EIMC采用早期协同范式，在本地融合阶段引入轻量级协同体素，生成紧凑且信息丰富的3D协同先验，从而增强跨模态对齐。随后，通过热图驱动的共识协议识别需要协作的区域，并仅查询低置信度、高差异区域内的Top-K实例向量进行跨注意力融合。最后，通过精炼融合进一步提升关键遮挡目标的检测效果。实验表明，EIMC在OPV2V和DAIR-V2X数据集上取得了73.01%的AP@0.5性能，同时将带宽使用量减少了87.98%。代码已公开。

item.title: 面向科学领域的动态多模型协同框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种科学领域动态多模型协同框架，提升复杂推理能力。



摘要： 本文针对现有多智能体大语言模型框架在科学和知识密集型领域表现不佳的问题，提出了一种面向科学领域的交互式双层多模型编排框架。该框架通过专用编排模型分析任务，动态构建领域感知的推理流程，并实例化具有定制提示的专家代理；执行模型则按照生成的角色和指令规范完成每一步操作。编排器根据中间反馈迭代更新流程，实现动态重规划、角色重新分配和提示优化，增强科学推理的鲁棒性和专业化。该框架支持异构大语言模型集成，具备灵活的性能与效率权衡能力，在多种推理和科学风格基准测试中均表现出色。

item.title: LLM时代下TeX的局限与Mogan STEM的替代方案
item.content:encoded:
本文分析TeX在LLM时代的不足并介绍Mogan STEM作为更优替代。



摘要： 随着大语言模型（LLMs）在科学写作中的广泛应用，TeX在编译效率、语义生成、错误定位和工具生态方面的缺陷日益凸显。本文深入分析了TeX的这些局限性，并提出Mogan STEM作为一种结构化编辑器的替代方案。Mogan通过高效的数据结构、快速渲染和按需插件加载，在多个方面优于TeX。实验结果表明，Mogan在编译/渲染时间和LLM任务性能上表现更佳。此外，由于Mogan文档格式.tmu具有更低的信息熵，它在微调LLM时更加高效。因此，文章呼吁开展更大规模的基于.tmu格式的LLM训练实验。

item.title: 多物体阴影生成方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种多物体阴影生成方法，提升图像合成效果。



摘要： 本文针对图像合成中多物体阴影生成的问题，提出了一种基于预训练文本到图像扩散模型的方法。该方法通过图像路径和文本路径协同工作，提供细粒度的空间引导并融合物体阴影的边界框信息，利用注意力对齐损失确保阴影区域的准确性。为支持该任务，作者扩展了DESOBAv2数据集，构建了包含多个插入物体的合成场景，并自动生成结合物体类别和阴影位置信息的提示。实验结果表明，该方法在单物体和多物体阴影生成任务中均取得了最佳性能。

item.title: 用于评估腹腔内软组织3D重建的D4D数据集
item.content:encoded:
D4D数据集提供手术条件下软组织3D重建的视频与几何数据。



摘要： D4D数据集包含从六只猪尸体实验中获取的立体内窥镜视频和高精度结构光几何数据，用于评估真实手术环境下腹部软组织的3D重建。数据通过光学跟踪和手动迭代对齐方法进行配准，包含三种序列类型以测试算法在非刚性运动、变形程度和视图外更新方面的鲁棒性。每段视频提供校正后的立体图像、工具掩码、深度信息、点云及相机姿态等数据。该数据集包含超过30万帧和369个点云，适用于非刚性SLAM、4D重建和深度估计方法的开发与评估。

item.title: 面向教育的AI生成视频质量评估基准与方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出首个教育类AI视频质量评估框架EduVQA，提升AI教学视频质量。



摘要： 本文针对AI生成视频在教育领域的应用潜力不足问题，提出了EduAIGV-1k数据集和评估框架，用于评估AI生成视频在教授基础数学概念方面的质量。该数据集包含1,130个由先进文本到视频模型生成的视频，每个视频配有精细标注，涵盖感知质量和提示对齐两个维度。基于此，作者引入了EduVQA系统，并提出S2D-MoE模块以提升整体质量评估效果。实验表明EduVQA优于现有方法，相关数据和代码将公开。

item.title: 可解释时间序列预测方法IPL的提出与应用
item.content:encoded:
本文提出可解释多项式学习方法，提升时间序列预测的可解释性。



摘要： 时间序列预测在资产性能管理中发挥重要作用，但现有方法缺乏可解释性，影响用户信任和调试效率。本文提出可解释多项式学习（IPL）方法，通过多项式表示显式建模原始特征及其交互，保留时间依赖性并提供特征级可解释性。该方法在模拟数据和比特币价格数据上表现出高精度和良好可解释性，并在实际天线数据中验证了其简单高效的早期预警能力。

item.title: StitchCUDA：面向端到端GPU程序生成的多智能体框架
item.content:encoded:
StitchCUDA提升GPU程序生成效率与性能。



摘要： 本文提出StitchCUDA，一个用于端到端GPU程序生成的多智能体框架，包含规划者、编码器和验证器三个核心组件。该框架通过基于规则的代理强化学习，提升了编码器在任务到代码生成和反馈驱动优化方面的能力，并有效防止了奖励滥用问题。实验表明，StitchCUDA在端到端GPU编程任务中成功率接近100%，比多智能体基线快1.72倍，比强化学习模型基线快2.73倍。

item.title: 基于NEMF框架的物理反演方法构建功能性数字孪生
item.content:encoded:
NEMF框架通过物理反演实现高精度材料参数重建，构建可模拟的数字孪生。



摘要： 本文提出NEMF框架，用于通过非接触式传感技术重建场景中的材料属性，从而构建功能性的数字孪生。传统方法如NeRF虽能生成视觉逼真的3D模型，但缺乏实际物理属性。NEMF通过解耦几何、环境场和材料属性，将原本病态的物理反演问题转化为物理监督的学习任务。该框架利用图像获取高保真几何信息，结合射频信号进行材料参数重建，实现了对场景中材料属性的连续空间分布建模。实验表明，该方法在合成数据集上具有高精度，能够生成可进行高保真物理仿真的数字孪生。

item.title: 基于LLM内部表示的回归预测统计量研究
item.content:encoded:
LLM内部表示可捕捉数值预测的统计特性，提升预测效率。



摘要： 本文研究了大型语言模型（LLMs）在回归任务中的应用，特别是其在连续值输出时的预测分布问题。传统方法依赖自回归解码，计算成本高。本文提出通过训练回归探针，直接从LLM的内部表示中预测统计量（如均值、中位数、分位数），发现LLM嵌入包含关于预测分布的有用信息，包括数值不确定性。该研究为减少采样依赖、提升不确定性感知预测提供了新思路。

item.title: 基于解耦系统的物体抓取框架Unveiler研究
item.content:encoded:
Unveiler通过解耦空间推理与动作执行提升复杂环境下的物体抓取效率。



摘要： 本文提出Unveiler框架，通过将高阶空间推理与低阶动作执行解耦，提升在密集杂乱环境中物体抓取的效率和成功率。核心是轻量级Transformer结构的空间关系编码器（SRE），用于逐步识别关键障碍物，再由旋转不变的动作解码器执行操作。SRE分两阶段训练：模仿学习提供高效初始化，PPO微调使策略超越传统启发式方法。实验表明，在模拟环境中，Unveiler在部分遮挡和完全遮挡场景中分别达到97.6%和90.0%的成功率，并且其空间推理能力可零样本迁移到真实场景，无需重新训练模型组件。

item.title: 分布式随机优化中的梯度延迟框架研究
item.content:encoded:
研究分布式随机优化中梯度延迟问题的解决方法。



摘要： 本文提出了一种适用于梯度延迟情况下的分布式随机优化通用框架。在该框架中，多个本地代理利用自身数据和计算能力协助中心服务器最小化全局目标函数。每个代理可以传输其局部梯度的随机估计值，这些估计可能带有偏差和延迟。尽管已有研究建议采用自适应步长来应对延迟，但本文证明固定递减步长同样有效，并且在非凸和强凸目标函数上能够达到最优SGD性能。

item.title: 基于函数ANOVA的模型可解释性方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新的函数ANOVA方法用于模型解释。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于函数ANOVA的模型可解释性方法，解决了依赖特征下模型预测分解的难题。通过结合函数分析与离散傅里叶分析，该方法在不假设分布形式的情况下，实现了对分类输入的闭式分解。该方法计算高效，能够无缝恢复独立情况下的经典结果，并扩展到任意依赖结构。同时，利用SHAP与ANOVA在独立情况下的联系，该框架自然推广了SHAP值至一般分类场景。

item.title: 基于知识库与逻辑推理的辩论分析框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种透明的辩论分析方法，结合论证挖掘与逻辑推理。



摘要： 本文针对大语言模型在复杂文本推理上的不足，提出一个整合学习型论证挖掘、定量推理和本体查询的框架。该框架从原始辩论文本中提取模糊论证知识库，将论证表示为实体，并通过攻击和支持关系进行连接，同时标注初始模糊强度。随后应用定量论证语义计算最终论证强度，并将其嵌入模糊描述逻辑中，实现高效的查询回答。该方法提供了一种透明、可解释且形式化的方法来分析辩论，弥补了传统统计方法的局限性。

item.title: AI代理行为多样性研究与角色建模
item.content:encoded:
通过角色建模分析AI代理的行为多样性。



摘要： 本文利用Persona Ecosystem Playground（PEP）对Moltbook平台上的41,300条帖子进行分析，通过k-means聚类和检索增强生成技术生成并验证了对话角色。跨角色验证显示，角色与其所属集群的语义相似度高于与其他集群的相似度。随后，在九轮结构化讨论中，模拟消息被显著地归因于其来源角色。研究结果表明，基于角色的生态系统建模可以有效表征AI代理群体中的行为多样性。

item.title: 基于轨迹一致性的Padé近似加速扩散模型采样
item.content:encoded:
提出TC-Padé方法提升扩散模型低步数采样效率。



摘要： 尽管扩散模型在生成质量上表现优异，但其迭代采样过程计算开销大。现有特征缓存技术在高步数下有效，但在低步数（20-30步）时效果受限。传统方法难以处理不同去噪阶段的动力学差异，且多项式外推器易产生误差累积。为此，本文提出轨迹一致的Padé近似（TC-Padé），通过有理函数建模特征演化，更准确捕捉渐近和过渡行为。该方法包含自适应系数调制和步长感知预测策略，显著提升采样效率。实验表明，在FLUX.1-dev和Wan2.1等模型上，TC-Padé实现2.88倍和1.72倍加速，同时保持高质量生成结果。

item.content:encoded:
提出ShipTraj-R1框架，提升船舶轨迹预测精度。



摘要： 本文提出ShipTraj-R1，一个基于大语言模型的船舶轨迹预测框架。通过将轨迹预测任务转化为文本生成问题，并引入动态提示和规则奖励机制，增强模型的推理能力。该框架采用GRPO优化方法，并以Qwen3为模型基础，在两个真实海事数据集上实验显示，其预测误差最小，优于现有深度学习和LLM基线方法。

item.title: 基于大语言模型的科学论文自动优化方法APRES
item.content:encoded:
APRES提升论文质量与影响力，增强科学传播效果。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于大语言模型（LLMs）的新型论文优化方法APRES，旨在通过自动化修订提升科学论文的质量和影响力。该方法利用评估量表预测未来引用次数，并据此对论文进行优化，同时保持核心科学内容不变。实验表明，APRES在平均误差上比现有最佳基线提高了19.6%，且79%的修订论文得到专家评委的青睐。研究强调APRES旨在辅助而非取代人类评审，帮助作者在投稿前更好地测试和改进其研究成果，从而推动科学进步。

item.title: 差分隐私下优化算法的收敛性分析
item.content:encoded:
研究差分隐私噪声对优化算法的影响，对比DP-SGD与DP-SignSGD性能。



摘要： 本文从随机微分方程的角度重新审视差分隐私噪声与优化自适应性的相互作用，首次对私有优化器进行了基于SDE的分析。重点比较了在每样本裁剪下的DP-SGD和DP-SignSGD，在固定超参数下，DP-SGD的隐私-效用权衡为O(1/ε²)，而DP-SignSGD的收敛速度与ε成线性关系，且权衡为O(1/ε)。当使用最优学习率时，两者理论表现相近，但DP-SGD的学习率与ε线性相关，而DP-SignSGD则基本不依赖ε。这表明自适应方法在实际应用中更具优势，因其超参数可跨隐私水平迁移。实验结果验证了理论，并扩展至DP-Adam。

item.title: NOVA：无需配对数据的视频编辑框架
item.content:encoded:
NOVA通过稀疏控制与密集合成实现高效视频编辑。



摘要： 本文提出NOVA，一种无需配对数据的视频编辑框架。该框架包含稀疏分支和密集分支，前者通过用户编辑的关键帧提供语义指导，后者则持续整合原始视频的运动和纹理信息以保持高保真和连贯性。此外，NOVA引入退化模拟训练策略，使模型在人工降噪视频上学习运动重建和时间一致性，从而摆脱对配对数据的依赖。实验表明，NOVA在编辑保真度、运动保留和时间一致性方面优于现有方法。

item.title: 基于LLM的零样本图推理自适应子图提取与去噪框架
item.content:encoded:
提出GraphSSR框架解决零样本图推理中的结构噪声问题。



摘要： 本文针对零样本图任务中因数据稀缺和传统GNN泛化能力不足的问题，提出GraphSSR框架，通过自适应子图提取和去噪提升基于大语言模型的图推理性能。该框架采用“采样-选择-推理”流程动态调整子图结构，减少无关邻接点和边的影响，并结合监督微调和强化学习策略优化推理过程，从而提高预测准确性。

item.title: SUN：跨模型解码执行的高效共享方法
item.content:encoded:
SUN实现跨模型解码共享，提升GPU利用率与系统吞吐。



摘要： 在多模型大语言模型服务中，由于模型特定的资源划分，解码执行效率低下，尤其在负载不均时导致GPU利用率不足。为此，研究提出SUN（Shared Use of Next-token Prediction），首次实现去中心化多LLM服务中的跨模型解码共享。SUN将解码器仅Transformer分解为预填充模块和解码模块，仅微调任务特定的预填充模块，使冻结的解码模块可跨模型共享。该设计支持模型无关的解码路由策略，平衡解码请求以最大化利用率。SUN在多种任务和模型家族中实现了与全微调相当的准确性，同时减少解码工作者数量并保持系统吞吐。实验显示，SUN在传统解耦架构上提升了2.0倍的每GPU吞吐量，且时间每输出标记（TPOT）保持在5%以内。此外，SUN还支持低比特解码，通过量化SUN（QSUN）实现45%的速度提升，同时保持共享解码的优势。

item.title: 多模态大语言模型在文档信息提取中的性能评估
item.content:encoded:
多模态大模型在文档信息提取中表现优异，可替代传统OCR流程。



摘要： 本文对多种多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）在商业文档信息提取任务中的性能进行了大规模基准测试。研究提出了一种自动化的层级错误分析框架，用于系统诊断模型的错误模式。实验结果表明，强大的MLLM在仅使用图像输入的情况下，可以达到与OCR增强方法相当的性能，说明OCR可能不再是必需步骤。此外，研究还发现精心设计的模式、示例和指令能够进一步提升MLLM的表现。该工作为文档信息提取技术的发展提供了实用指导和重要见解。

item.title: 视频生成中的可扩展盲水印框架SIGMark
item.content:encoded:
提出SIGMark实现高效无损视频水印，提升AI安全。



摘要： 本文提出SIGMark，一种适用于视频扩散模型的可扩展、无盲提取的水印框架。与传统方法不同，SIGMark通过生成全局帧级伪随机编码初始噪声，降低存储成本并保持噪声分布和多样性，从而实现无损水印。此外，设计了针对因果3D变分自编码器的段组排序模块，增强对时间扰动的鲁棒性。实验表明，SIGMark在时空扰动下仍能保持高比特准确率，具有良好的可扩展性和实用性。

item.title: 跨模型推测预填充在代理大语言模型中的应用
item.content:encoded:
跨模型推测预填充可有效压缩提示，提升推理效率。



摘要： 本文研究了跨模型推测预填充技术，利用不同模型家族中的轻量级草稿模型对目标模型进行提示压缩。实验表明，尽管模型架构和分词器存在差异，基于注意力的标记重要性估计仍能可靠迁移。该方法在多种任务中保持了90~100%的基准性能，甚至在某些情况下因去噪效果而略微提升准确率，同时显著减少了首次token生成时间。这表明推测预填充主要依赖任务先验和语义结构，具有良好的泛化能力，适用于需要多模型协作的代理系统。

item.title: SynthCharge：生成可验证的电动汽车路径规划基准数据集
item.content:encoded:
SynthCharge生成可验证的EVRPTW实例，支持动态评估模型性能。



摘要： 本文提出SynthCharge，一个参数化生成器，用于创建多样化的电动汽车路径规划问题实例。该生成器考虑了电池容量和充电站位置，并通过可行性筛选确保实例可解。相比静态基准数据集，SynthCharge支持不同规模和时空配置的实例生成，为学习型路径规划模型提供动态评估平台。实验聚焦于5至100个客户的实例，强调其在神经路由和数据驱动方法中的应用价值。

item.title: Valet：用于评估不完全信息博弈算法的测试平台
item.content:encoded:
Valet是一个用于评估不完全信息博弈算法的多样化测试平台。



摘要： 本文介绍了Valet，一个包含21种传统不完全信息纸牌游戏的综合性测试平台，旨在促进不完全信息博弈算法的研究与比较。这些游戏涵盖多种类型、文化背景和规则机制。为了统一实现，所有游戏规则均以RECYCLE语言编码。通过随机模拟，作者分析了每款游戏的分支因子和持续时间，并提供了蒙特卡洛树搜索玩家对随机对手的基准分数分布，证明了Valet作为基准测试套件的有效性。

item.title: SSD：一种加速自回归解码的并行方法
item.content:encoded:
SSD通过并行化推测与验证提升解码效率。



摘要： 文章提出了一种名为SSD（Speculative Speculative Decoding）的新型解码方法，旨在解决传统自回归解码和推测解码中的序列依赖问题。SSD通过在验证过程中提前预测可能的验证结果，并预先生成推测内容，从而在实际验证结果匹配时直接返回推测结果，大幅减少推理时间。研究识别了SSD面临的三个关键挑战，并提出了相应的解决方案，最终实现了一个优化的算法Saguaro。实验表明，该方法在开源推理引擎中比优化的推测解码基线快2倍，比自回归解码快5倍。

item.title: 混合架构在上下文检索任务中的性能比较研究
item.content:encoded:
混合模型在数据效率和信息密集型检索中表现优于SSMs。



摘要： 本文研究了结合Transformer和状态空间模型（SSM）的混合架构在两种合成上下文检索任务中的表现。第一项任务是n-gram检索，第二项是位置检索。实验表明，混合模型在数据效率和信息密集型任务中优于SSMs，并接近Transformer的表现，但在位置检索上仍不如Transformer。分析发现，SSM模型生成的嵌入具有局部性特征，这解释了其在不同任务中的优劣势。研究为模型选择提供了理论依据。

item.title: Heterogeneous Agent Collaborative Reinforcement Learning
item.content:encoded: We introduce Heterogeneous Agent Collaborative Reinforcement Learning (HACRL), a new learning paradigm that addresses the inefficiencies of isolated on-policy optimization. HACRL enables collaborative optimization with independent execution: heterogeneous agents share verified rollouts during training to mutually improve, while operating independently at inference time. Unlike LLM-based multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL), HACRL does not require coordinated deployment, and unlike on-/off-policy distillation, it enables bidirectional mutual learning among heterogeneous agents rather than one-directional teacher-to-student transfer. Building on this paradigm, we propose HACPO, a collaborative RL algorithm that enables principled rollout sharing to maximize sample utilization and cross-agent knowledge transfer. To mitigate capability discrepancies and policy distribution shifts, HACPO introduces four tailored mechanisms with theoretical guarantees on unbiased advantage estimation and optimization correctness. Extensive experiments across diverse heterogeneous model combinations and reasoning benchmarks show that HACPO consistently improves all participating agents, outperforming GSPO by an average of 3.3\% while using only half the rollout cost.

item.title: A Natural Language Agentic Approach to Study Affective Polarization
item.content:encoded: Affective polarization has been central to political and social studies, with growing focus on social media, where partisan divisions are often exacerbated. Real-world studies tend to have limited scope, while simulated studies suffer from insufficient high-quality training data, as manually labeling posts is labor-intensive and prone to subjective biases. The lack of adequate tools to formalize different definitions of affective polarization across studies complicates result comparison and hinders interoperable frameworks. We present a multi-agent model providing a comprehensive approach to studying affective polarization in social media. To operationalize our framework, we develop a platform leveraging large language models (LLMs) to construct virtual communities where agents engage in discussions. We showcase the potential of our platform by (1) analyzing questions related to affective polarization, as explored in social science literature, providing a fresh perspective on this phenomenon, and (2) introducing scenarios that allow observation and measurement of polarization at different levels of granularity and abstraction. Experiments show that our platform is a flexible tool for computational studies of complex social dynamics such as affective polarization. It leverages advanced agent models to simulate rich, context-sensitive interactions and systematically explore research questions traditionally addressed through human-subject studies.

item.title: See and Remember: A Multimodal Agent for Web Traversal
item.content:encoded: Autonomous web navigation requires agents to perceive complex visual environments and maintain long-term context, yet current Large Language Model (LLM) based agents often struggle with spatial disorientation and navigation loops. In this paper, we propose generally applicable V-GEMS(Visual Grounding and Explicit Memory System), a robust multimodal agent architecture designed for precise and resilient web traversal. Our agent integrates visual grounding to resolve ambiguous interactive elements and introduces an explicit memory stack with state tracking. This dual mechanism allows the agent to maintain a structured map of its traversal path, enabling valid backtracking and preventing cyclical failures in deep navigation tasks. We also introduce an updatable dynamic benchmark to rigorously evaluate adaptability. Experiments show V-GEMS significantly dominates the WebWalker baseline, achieving a substantial 28.7% performance gain. Code is available at https://github.com/Vaultttttttttttt/V-GEMS.

item.title: HiLoRA: Hierarchical Low-Rank Adaptation for Personalized Federated Learning
item.content:encoded: Vision Transformers (ViTs) have been widely adopted in vision tasks due to their strong transferability. In Federated Learning (FL), where full fine-tuning is communication heavy, Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) provides an efficient and communication-friendly way to adapt ViTs. However, existing LoRA-based federated tuning methods overlook latent client structures in real-world settings, limiting shared representation learning and hindering effective adaptation to unseen clients. To address this, we propose HiLoRA, a hierarchical LoRA framework that places adapters at three levels: root, cluster, and leaf, each designed to capture global, subgroup, and client-specific knowledge, respectively. Through cross-tier orthogonality and cascaded optimization, HiLoRA separates update subspaces and aligns each tier with its residual personalized objective. In particular, we develop a LoRA-Subspace Adaptive Clustering mechanism that infers latent client groups via subspace similarity analysis, thereby facilitating knowledge sharing across structurally aligned clients. Theoretically, we establish a tier-wise generalization analysis that supports HiLoRA's design. Experiments on ViT backbones with CIFAR-100 and DomainNet demonstrate consistent improvements in both personalization and generalization.

item.title: 3D-DRES: Detailed 3D Referring Expression Segmentation
item.content:encoded: Current 3D visual grounding tasks only process sentence level detection or segmentation, which critically fails to leverage the rich compositional contextual reasonings within natural language expressions. To address this challenge, we introduce Detailed 3D Referring Expression Segmentation (3D-DRES), a new task that provides a phrase to 3D instance mapping, aiming at enhancing fine-grained 3D vision language understanding. To support 3D-DRES, we present DetailRefer, a new dataset comprising 54,432 descriptions spanning 11,054 distinct objects. Unlike previous datasets, DetailRefer implements a pioneering phrase-instance annotation paradigm where each referenced noun phrase is explicitly mapped to its corresponding 3D elements. Additionally, we introduce DetailBase, a purposefully streamlined yet effective baseline architecture that supports dual-mode segmentation at both sentence and phrase levels. Our experimental results demonstrate that models trained on DetailRefer not only excel at phrase-level segmentation but also show surprising improvements on traditional 3D-RES benchmarks.

item.title: Why Does RLAIF Work At All?
item.content:encoded: Reinforcement Learning from AI Feedback (RLAIF) enables language models to improve by training on their own preference judgments, yet no theoretical account explains why this self-improvement seemingly works for value learning. We propose the latent value hypothesis, that pretraining on internet-scale data encodes human values as directions in representation space, and constitutional prompts elicit these latent values into preference judgments. We formalize this intuition under a linear model where the constitution acts as a projection operator selecting value-relevant directions. Our analysis yields several results. RLAIF improves alignment when the constitution-activated direction correlates with true values better than the model's default generation direction thus explaining the generation-judgment gap; the ceiling on RLAIF quality is determined by how well representations encode values, which scales with model capacity; and adversarial constitutions exist that can activate anti-social value directions encoded from harmful pretraining data. Our account unifies scattered empirical findings including the refusal direction, low-rank safety subspaces, and RLAIF scaling behavior.

item.title: Gravity Falls: A Comparative Analysis of Domain-Generation Algorithm (DGA) Detection Methods for Mobile Device Spearphishing
item.content:encoded: Mobile devices are frequent targets of eCrime threat actors through SMS spearphishing (smishing) links that leverage Domain Generation Algorithms (DGA) to rotate hostile infrastructure. Despite this, DGA research and evaluation largely emphasize malware C2 and email phishing datasets, leaving limited evidence on how well detectors generalize to smishing-driven domain tactics outside enterprise perimeters. This work addresses that gap by evaluating traditional and machine-learning DGA detectors against Gravity Falls, a new semi-synthetic dataset derived from smishing links delivered between 2022 and 2025. Gravity Falls captures a single threat actor's evolution across four technique clusters, shifting from short randomized strings to dictionary concatenation and themed combo-squatting variants used for credential theft and fee/fine fraud. Two string-analysis approaches (Shannon entropy and Exp0se) and two ML-based detectors (an LSTM classifier and COSSAS DGAD) are assessed using Top-1M domains as benign baselines. Results are strongly tactic-dependent: performance is highest on randomized-string domains but drops on dictionary concatenation and themed combo-squatting, with low recall across multiple tool/cluster pairings. Overall, both traditional heuristics and recent ML detectors are ill-suited for consistently evolving DGA tactics observed in Gravity Falls, motivating more context-aware approaches and providing a reproducible benchmark for future evaluation.

item.title: Semi-Supervised Few-Shot Adaptation of Vision-Language Models
item.content:encoded: Vision-language models (VLMs) pre-trained on large, heterogeneous data sources are becoming increasingly popular, providing rich multi-modal embeddings that enable efficient transfer to new tasks. A particularly relevant application is few-shot adaptation, where only a handful of annotated examples are available to adapt the model through multi-modal linear probes. In medical imaging, specialized VLMs have shown promising performance in zero- and few-shot image classification, which is valuable for mitigating the high cost of expert annotations. However, challenges remain in extremely low-shot regimes: the inherent class imbalances in medical tasks often lead to underrepresented categories, penalizing overall model performance. To address this limitation, we propose leveraging unlabeled data by introducing an efficient semi-supervised solver that propagates text-informed pseudo-labels during few-shot adaptation. The proposed method enables lower-budget annotation pipelines for adapting VLMs, reducing labeling effort by >50% in low-shot regimes.

item.title: Beyond Factual Correctness: Mitigating Preference-Inconsistent Explanations in Explainable Recommendation
item.content:encoded: LLM-based explainable recommenders can produce fluent explanations that are factually correct, yet still justify items using attributes that conflict with a user's historical preferences. Such preference-inconsistent explanations yield logically valid but unconvincing reasoning and are largely missed by standard hallucination or faithfulness metrics. We formalize this failure mode and propose PURE, a preference-aware reasoning framework following a select-then-generate paradigm. Instead of only improving generation, PURE intervenes in evidence selection, it selects a compact set of multi-hop item-centric reasoning paths that are both factually grounded and aligned with user preference structure, guided by user intent, specificity, and diversity to suppress generic, weakly personalized evidence. The selected evidence is then injected into LLM generation via structure-aware prompting that preserves relational constraints. To measure preference inconsistency, we introduce a feature-level, user-centric evaluation metric that reveals misalignment overlooked by factuality-based measures. Experiments on three real-world datasets show that PURE consistently reduces preference-inconsistent explanations and factual hallucinations while maintaining competitive recommendation accuracy, explanation quality, and inference efficiency. These results highlight that trustworthy explanations require not only factual correctness but also justification aligned with user preferences.

item.title: Chain of World: World Model Thinking in Latent Motion
item.content:encoded: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models are a promising path toward embodied intelligence, yet they often overlook the predictive and temporal-causal structure underlying visual dynamics. World-model VLAs address this by predicting future frames, but waste capacity reconstructing redundant backgrounds. Latent-action VLAs encode frame-to-frame transitions compactly, but lack temporally continuous dynamic modeling and world knowledge. To overcome these limitations, we introduce CoWVLA (Chain-of-World VLA), a new "Chain of World" paradigm that unifies world-model temporal reasoning with a disentangled latent motion representation. First, a pretrained video VAE serves as a latent motion extractor, explicitly factorizing video segments into structure and motion latents. Then, during pre-training, the VLA learns from an instruction and an initial frame to infer a continuous latent motion chain and predict the segment's terminal frame. Finally, during co-fine-tuning, this latent dynamic is aligned with discrete action prediction by jointly modeling sparse keyframes and action sequences in a unified autoregressive decoder. This design preserves the world-model benefits of temporal reasoning and world knowledge while retaining the compactness and interpretability of latent actions, enabling efficient visuomotor learning. Extensive experiments on robotic simulation benchmarks show that CoWVLA outperforms existing world-model and latent-action approaches and achieves moderate computational efficiency, highlighting its potential as a more effective VLA pretraining paradigm. The project website can be found at https://fx-hit.github.io/cowvla-io.

item.title: Safe and Robust Domains of Attraction for Discrete-Time Systems: A Set-Based Characterization and Certifiable Neural Network Estimation
item.content:encoded: Analyzing nonlinear systems with attracting robust invariant sets (RISs) requires estimating their domains of attraction (DOAs). Despite extensive research, accurately characterizing DOAs for general nonlinear systems remains challenging due to both theoretical and computational limitations, particularly in the presence of uncertainties and state constraints. In this paper, we propose a novel framework for the accurate estimation of safe (state-constrained) and robust DOAs for discrete-time nonlinear uncertain systems with continuous dynamics, open safe sets, compact disturbance sets, and uniformly locally $\ell_p$-stable compact RISs. The notion of uniform $\ell_p$ stability is quite general and encompasses, as special cases, uniform exponential and polynomial stability. The DOAs are characterized via newly introduced value functions defined on metric spaces of compact sets. We establish their fundamental mathematical properties and derive the associated Bellman-type (Zubov-type) functional equations. Building on this characterization, we develop a physics-informed neural network (NN) framework to learn the corresponding value functions by embedding the derived Bellman-type equations directly into the training process. To obtain certifiable estimates of the safe robust DOAs from the learned neural approximations, we further introduce a verification procedure that leverages existing formal verification tools. The effectiveness and applicability of the proposed methodology are demonstrated through four numerical examples involving nonlinear uncertain systems subject to state constraints, and its performance is compared with existing methods from the literature.

item.title: Uni-Skill: Building Self-Evolving Skill Repository for Generalizable Robotic Manipulation
item.content:encoded: While skill-centric approaches leverage foundation models to enhance generalization in compositional tasks, they often rely on fixed skill libraries, limiting adaptability to new tasks without manual intervention. To address this, we propose Uni-Skill, a Unified Skill-centric framework that supports skill-aware planning and facilitates automatic skill evolution. Unlike prior methods that restrict planning to predefined skills, Uni-Skill requests for new skill implementations when existing ones are insufficient, ensuring adaptable planning with self-augmented skill library. To support automatic implementation of diverse skills requested by the planning module, we construct SkillFolder, a VerbNet-inspired repository derived from large-scale unstructured robotic videos. SkillFolder introduces a hierarchical skill taxonomy that captures diverse skill descriptions at multiple levels of abstraction. By populating this taxonomy with large-scale, automatically annotated demonstrations, Uni-Skill shifts the paradigm of skill acquisition from inefficient manual annotation to efficient offline structural retrieval. Retrieved examples provide semantic supervision over behavior patterns and fine-grained references for spatial trajectories, enabling few-shot skill inference without deployment-time demonstrations. Comprehensive experiments in both simulation and real-world settings verify the state-of-the-art performance of Uni-Skill over existing VLM-based skill-centric approaches, highlighting its advanced reasoning capabilities and strong zero-shot generalization across a wide range of novel tasks.

item.title: On the Expressive Power of Transformers for Maxout Networks and Continuous Piecewise Linear Functions
item.content:encoded: Transformer networks have achieved remarkable empirical success across a wide range of applications, yet their theoretical expressive power remains insufficiently understood. In this paper, we study the expressive capabilities of Transformer architectures. We first establish an explicit approximation of maxout networks by Transformer networks while preserving comparable model complexity. As a consequence, Transformers inherit the universal approximation capability of ReLU networks under similar complexity constraints. Building on this connection, we develop a framework to analyze the approximation of continuous piecewise linear functions by Transformers and quantitatively characterize their expressivity via the number of linear regions, which grows exponentially with depth. Our analysis establishes a theoretical bridge between approximation theory for standard feedforward neural networks and Transformer architectures. It also yields structural insights into Transformers: self-attention layers implement max-type operations, while feedforward layers realize token-wise affine transformations.

item.title: CoFL: Continuous Flow Fields for Language-Conditioned Navigation
item.content:encoded: Language-conditioned navigation pipelines often rely on brittle modular components or costly action-sequence generation. To address these limitations, we present CoFL, an end-to-end policy that directly maps a bird's-eye view (BEV) observation and a language instruction to a continuous flow field for navigation. Instead of predicting discrete action tokens or sampling action chunks via iterative denoising, CoFL outputs instantaneous velocities that can be queried at arbitrary 2D projected locations. Trajectories are obtained by numerical integration of the predicted field, producing smooth motion that remains reactive under closed-loop execution. To enable large-scale training, we build a dataset of over 500k BEV image-instruction pairs, each procedurally annotated with a flow field and a trajectory derived from BEV semantic maps built on Matterport3D and ScanNet. By training on a mixed distribution, CoFL significantly outperforms modular Vision-Language Model (VLM)-based planners and generative policy baselines on strictly unseen scenes. Finally, we deploy CoFL zero-shot in real-world experiments with overhead BEV observations across multiple layouts, maintaining reliable closed-loop control and a high success rate.

item.title: On Discriminative vs. Generative classifiers: Rethinking MLLMs for Action Understanding
item.content:encoded: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have advanced open-world action understanding and can be adapted as generative classifiers for closed-set settings by autoregressively generating action labels as text. However, this approach is inefficient, and shared subwords across action labels introduce semantic overlap, leading to ambiguity in generation. In contrast, discriminative classifiers learn task-specific representations with clear decision boundaries, enabling efficient one-step classification without autoregressive decoding. We first compare generative and discriminative classifiers with MLLMs for closed-set action understanding, revealing the superior accuracy and efficiency of the latter. To bridge the performance gap, we design strategies that elevate generative classifiers toward performance comparable with discriminative ones. Furthermore, we show that generative modeling can complement discriminative classifiers, leading to better performance while preserving efficiency. To this end, we propose Generation-Assisted Discriminative~(GAD) classifier for closed-set action understanding. GAD operates only during fine-tuning, preserving full compatibility with MLLM pretraining. Extensive experiments on temporal action understanding benchmarks demonstrate that GAD improves both accuracy and efficiency over generative methods, achieving state-of-the-art results on four tasks across five datasets, including an average 2.5% accuracy gain and 3x faster inference on our largest COIN benchmark.

item.title: Credibility Governance: A Social Mechanism for Collective Self-Correction under Weak Truth Signals
item.content:encoded: Online platforms increasingly rely on opinion aggregation to allocate real-world attention and resources, yet common signals such as engagement votes or capital-weighted commitments are easy to amplify and often track visibility rather than reliability. This makes collective judgments brittle under weak truth signals, noisy or delayed feedback, early popularity surges, and strategic manipulation. We propose Credibility Governance (CG), a mechanism that reallocates influence by learning which agents and viewpoints consistently track evolving public evidence. CG maintains dynamic credibility scores for both agents and opinions, updates opinion influence via credibility-weighted endorsements, and updates agent credibility based on the long-run performance of the opinions they support, rewarding early and persistent alignment with emerging evidence while filtering short-lived noise. We evaluate CG in POLIS, a socio-physical simulation environment that models coupled belief dynamics and downstream feedback under uncertainty. Across settings with initial majority misalignment, observation noise and contamination, and misinformation shocks, CG outperforms vote-based, stake-weighted, and no-governance baselines, yielding faster recovery to the true state, reduced lock-in and path dependence, and improved robustness under adversarial pressure. Our implementation and experimental scripts are publicly available at https://github.com/Wanying-He/Credibility_Governance.

item.title: cPNN: Continuous Progressive Neural Networks for Evolving Streaming Time Series
item.content:encoded: Dealing with an unbounded data stream involves overcoming the assumption that data is identically distributed and independent. A data stream can, in fact, exhibit temporal dependencies (i.e., be a time series), and data can change distribution over time (concept drift). The two problems are deeply discussed, and existing solutions address them separately: a joint solution is absent. In addition, learning multiple concepts implies remembering the past (a.k.a. avoiding catastrophic forgetting in Neural Networks' terminology). This work proposes Continuous Progressive Neural Networks (cPNN), a solution that tames concept drifts, handles temporal dependencies, and bypasses catastrophic forgetting. cPNN is a continuous version of Progressive Neural Networks, a methodology for remembering old concepts and transferring past knowledge to fit the new concepts quickly. We base our method on Recurrent Neural Networks and exploit the Stochastic Gradient Descent applied to data streams with temporal dependencies. Results of an ablation study show a quick adaptation of cPNN to new concepts and robustness to drifts.

item.title: QFlowNet: Fast, Diverse, and Efficient Unitary Synthesis with Generative Flow Networks
item.content:encoded: Unitary Synthesis, the decomposition of a unitary matrix into a sequence of quantum gates, is a fundamental challenge in quantum compilation. Prevailing reinforcement learning(RL) approaches are often hampered by sparse reward signals, which necessitate complex reward shaping or long training times, and typically converge to a single policy, lacking solution diversity. In this work, we propose QFlowNet, a novel framework that learns efficiently from sparse signals by pairing a Generative Flow Network (GFlowNet) with Transformers. Our approach addresses two key challenges. First, the GFlowNet framework is fundamentally designed to learn a diverse policy that samples solutions proportional to their reward, overcoming the single-solution limitation of RL while offering faster inference than other generative models like diffusion. Second, the Transformers act as a powerful encoder, capturing the non-local structure of unitary matrices and compressing a high-dimensional state into a dense latent representation for the policy network. Our agent achieves an overall success rate of 99.7% on a 3-qubit benchmark(lengths 1-12) and discovers a diverse set of compact circuits, establishing QFlowNet as an efficient and diverse paradigm for unitary synthesis.

item.title: Mind the Way You Select Negative Texts: Pursuing the Distance Consistency in OOD Detection with VLMs
item.content:encoded: Out-of-distribution (OOD) detection seeks to identify samples from unknown classes, a critical capability for deploying machine learning models in open-world scenarios. Recent research has demonstrated that Vision-Language Models (VLMs) can effectively leverage their multi-modal representations for OOD detection. However, current methods often incorporate intra-modal distance during OOD detection, such as comparing negative texts with ID labels or comparing test images with image proxies. This design paradigm creates an inherent inconsistency against the inter-modal distance that CLIP-like VLMs are optimized for, potentially leading to suboptimal performance. To address this limitation, we propose InterNeg, a simple yet effective framework that systematically utilizes consistent inter-modal distance enhancement from textual and visual perspectives. From the textual perspective, we devise an inter-modal criterion for selecting negative texts. From the visual perspective, we dynamically identify high-confidence OOD images and invert them into the textual space, generating extra negative text embeddings guided by inter-modal distance. Extensive experiments across multiple benchmarks demonstrate the superiority of our approach. Notably, our InterNeg achieves state-of-the-art performance compared to existing works, with a 3.47\% reduction in FPR95 on the large-scale ImageNet benchmark and a 5.50\% improvement in AUROC on the challenging Near-OOD benchmark.

item.title: Rethinking Time Series Domain Generalization via Structure-Stratified Calibration
item.content:encoded: For time series arising from latent dynamical systems, existing cross-domain generalization methods commonly assume that samples are comparably meaningful within a shared representation space. In real-world settings, however, different datasets often originate from structurally heterogeneous families of dynamical systems, leading to fundamentally distinct feature distributions. Under such circumstances, performing global alignment while neglecting structural differences is highly prone to establishing spurious correspondences and inducing negative transfer. From the new perspective of cross-domain structural correspondence failure, we revisit this problem and propose a structurally stratified calibration framework. This approach explicitly distinguishes structurally consistent samples and performs amplitude calibration exclusively within structurally compatible sample clusters, thereby effectively alleviating generalization failures caused by structural incompatibility. Notably, the proposed framework achieves substantial performance improvements through a concise and computationally efficient calibration strategy. Evaluations on 19 public datasets (100.3k samples) demonstrate that SSCF significantly outperforms strong baselines under the zero-shot setting. These results confirm that establishing structural consistency prior to alignment constitutes a more reliable and effective pathway for improving cross-domain generalization of time series governed by latent dynamical systems.

item.title: 基于背景的隐私保护框架PPEDCRF在自动驾驶视频中的应用
item.content:encoded:
PPEDCRF有效保护驾驶视频中的位置隐私，同时保持检测性能。



摘要： 本文研究了通过背景视觉线索进行位置隐私泄露的问题，并提出了一种名为PPEDCRF的隐私保护框架。该框架通过动态条件随机场、归一化控制惩罚和实用噪声注入模块，在不影响目标检测与分割性能的前提下，对敏感背景区域进行精确扰动，从而有效降低位置检索攻击的成功率。实验表明，PPEDCRF在多个公开数据集上表现优于传统方法。

item.title: LLM代理自动微调的探索与实验
item.content:encoded:
LLM代理可有效实现自动化微调，提升模型性能。



摘要： 文章探讨了使用基于大语言模型的代理来实现端到端的模型微调，以解决传统方法中耗时且昂贵的问题。研究提出FT-Dojo环境和FT-Agent系统，通过评估驱动的反馈机制，使代理能够自主优化数据处理、训练流程和策略。实验表明，专门设计的微调代理在多个任务中表现优于通用方法，尤其在10项任务中取得最佳成绩。研究还揭示了代理在因果推理方面的局限性，展示了自动化微调的潜力与挑战。

item.title: ASTRA-bench：评估下一代AI在复杂上下文与多步骤推理中的表现
item.content:encoded:
ASTRA-bench评估AI在复杂个人上下文和多步骤任务中的表现。



摘要： 文章介绍了ASTRA-bench，这是一个新的基准测试，用于评估AI在处理大量个人数据、多样化工具和多步骤推理任务中的能力。该基准结合了时间演化的个人上下文、交互式工具箱和复杂用户意图，生成了2413个场景。实验表明，当前最先进的模型在高复杂度环境下表现不佳，尤其是在生成论点方面存在瓶颈。研究揭示了现有AI助手在处理混乱个人上下文和制定可靠多步骤计划方面的局限性，并提供了完整的执行环境和评估脚本以支持开发更智能的AI助手。

item.title: AI内容审核的挑战：共存违规与动态规则下的判断困境
item.content:encoded:
AI在处理多违规和动态规则时面临判断能力下降的问题。



摘要： 文章探讨了AI在在线内容审核中的核心挑战，特别是在处理共存违规（如同时违反偏见和人身攻击政策）以及动态规则（随上下文变化的平台指南）时的表现。尽管大语言模型在遵循固定规则方面表现良好，但在面对不稳定的政策或情境依赖的判断时，其能力明显下降，导致审核结果不一致，可能错误限制合法表达或放任有害内容。这引发了对现有静态基准测试是否能有效评估AI在现实场景中判断能力的质疑。

item.title: Mix-GRM：通过结构化推理机制提升生成奖励模型性能
item.content:encoded:
Mix-GRM通过结构化B-CoT和D-CoT提升奖励模型性能。



摘要： 本文提出Mix-GRM框架，通过模块化合成流程将原始推理转化为结构化的B-CoT（多维原则覆盖）和D-CoT（实质性判断严谨性），并结合SFT和RLVR优化推理机制。实验表明，Mix-GRM在五个基准测试中达到新最佳，平均优于领先开源模型8.2%。研究发现B-CoT适用于主观偏好任务，D-CoT更擅长客观正确性任务，且RLVR能引导模型根据任务需求自动调整推理风格。相关数据和代码已开源。

item.title: mtLoRA：解决多任务LoRA扩展性能下降的高效方法
item.content:encoded:
mtLoRA提升多任务LoRA性能，减少参数和训练时间。



摘要： 文章指出，当将多任务低秩适配（LoRA）扩展到大量任务时，会出现严重的性能下降，如在DOTA数据集上从88.2%准确率降至2.0%。这种失败源于参数和表示的不一致。现有解决方案如正则化和动态路由在大规模应用中失效，因为它们面临一个根本性权衡：增强正则化以减少任务间冲突会抑制有效路由所需的特征区分度。本文分析了这一权衡的两个根源：一是统一正则化破坏了任务间知识共享，二是组件级LoRA加剧了梯度冲突。基于这些发现，作者提出mtLoRA，包含三个创新设计：谱感知正则化、块级适配和细粒度路由。在四个大规模视觉和NLP基准测试中，mtLoRA表现出色，平均优于现有技术2.3%，同时使用更少参数和训练时间。

item.title: FLANS at SemEval-2026 Task 7: RAG with Open-Sourced Smaller LLMs for Everyday Knowledge Across Diverse Languages and Cultures
item.content:encoded: This system paper describes our participation in the SemEval-2025 Task-7 ``Everyday Knowledge Across Diverse Languages and Cultures''. We attended two subtasks, i.e., Track 1: Short Answer Questions (SAQ), and Track 2: Multiple-Choice Questions (MCQ). The methods we used are retrieval augmented generation (RAGs) with open-sourced smaller LLMs (OS-sLLMs). To better adapt to this shared task, we created our own culturally aware knowledge base (CulKBs) by extracting Wikipedia content using keyword lists we prepared. We extracted both culturally-aware wiki-text and country-specific wiki-summary. In addition to the local CulKBs, we also have one system integrating live online search output via DuckDuckGo. Towards better privacy and sustainability, we aimed to deploy smaller LLMs (sLLMs) that are open-sourced on the Ollama platform. We share the prompts we developed using refinement techniques and report the learning curve of such prompts. The tested languages are English, Spanish, and Chinese for both tracks. Our resources and codes are shared via https://github.com/aaronlifenghan/FLANS-2026

item.title: 基于多粒度序列建模的微视频推荐方法 MealRec
item.content:encoded:
提出 MealRec 解决微视频推荐中的噪声与模态冲突问题。



摘要： 本文针对微视频推荐中因多模态内容噪声和不可靠隐式反馈导致的用户偏好预测困难，提出一种基于多粒度序列建模的推荐方法 MealRec。该方法通过引入时序引导的内容扩散（TCD）和无噪声偏好去噪（NPD），在视频内部和跨视频层面捕捉时间相关性，提升视频表示质量和用户兴趣建模的准确性。实验表明，MealRec在多个平台的数据集上表现出色，具有良好的通用性和鲁棒性。

item.title: 基于图神经网络的对称性一致力场模型用于大尺度晶格系统模拟
item.content:encoded:
图神经网络实现高效、对称性一致的晶格动力学模拟。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于图神经网络（GNN）的力场框架，用于模拟晶格系统的绝热动力学。与传统方法相比，GNN通过局部信息传递和权重共享直接实现晶格对称性，简化了模型设计。该框架在半经典Holstein模型中进行了验证，训练数据来自精确对角化结果，展现出高力精度、线性扩展性和对大规模晶格的良好迁移能力。利用其可扩展性，研究者进行了大尺度朗之万模拟，揭示了电荷密度波有序化过程中的动态标度和异常缓慢的亚Allen-Cahn粗化现象。结果表明，GNN是处理关联晶格系统大规模动力学模拟的一种优雅且高效的架构。

item.title: 流式设置下广义线性预测的动量加速算法
item.content:encoded:
提出首个结合动量的流式优化算法，实现双动量加速。



摘要： 本文研究在流式设置下的广义线性预测问题，其中每次迭代仅使用一个新数据点进行梯度更新。尽管动量在确定性优化中已被广泛使用，但其在单次非二次随机优化中的加速效果仍是未解难题。本文提出了首个成功通过新颖的数据依赖邻近方法引入动量的算法，实现了双动量加速。所推导的过量风险界限分解为三部分：改进的优化误差、最小最大最优统计误差和更高阶的模型不匹配误差。证明通过精细的内更新平稳分析处理模型不匹配，并通过两阶段外循环分析定位统计误差。最终解决了Jain等人（2018a）提出的开放问题，证明动量加速在流式设置下的广义线性预测中比方差减少更有效。

item.title: 面向Society 5.0的智能家庭模拟器代理框架研究
item.content:encoded:
提出S5-HES Agent，实现智能家庭模拟的自动化与多领域适配。



摘要： 本文针对Society 5.0愿景下智能家庭技术发展需求，提出一种基于AI的智能家庭模拟器代理框架（S5-HES Agent）。该框架通过可互换的大语言模型实现自然语言驱动的端到端模拟配置，无需编程技能。采用检索增强生成（RAG）管道，结合语义、关键词和混合搜索方式获取智能家庭知识。实验表明，该框架在设备行为模拟、威胁场景匹配和可扩展性方面表现优异，为安全、能源、健康等多领域研究提供了稳定支持。

item.title: LinkVLA：提升自动驾驶中语言与动作对齐的新型架构
item.content:encoded:
LinkVLA提升自动驾驶中语言与动作对齐和效率。



摘要： 本文提出LinkVLA，一种用于增强自动驾驶中语言与动作对齐和生成效率的新架构。通过将语言和动作标记统一到共享离散代码本中，并引入辅助动作理解目标以建立双向语言-动作映射，从而提高跨模态一致性。同时，采用两步粗到细的生成方法（C2F）替代传统逐步生成，显著提升推理速度。实验表明，该方法在闭环驾驶基准测试中提升了指令遵循准确率和驾驶性能，同时降低了延迟。

item.title: 差分隐私下基于似然的统计推断方法
item.content:encoded:
本文提出一种差分隐私下基于充分统计量的统计推断方法。



摘要： 本文研究了差分隐私数据发布系统中统计推断的问题，提出了一种在指数族模型下释放差分隐私充分统计量的方法，并通过噪声校准的似然推断进行后续分析。作者提出了近似差分隐私释放截断充分统计量的通用方法，证明了插件式差分隐私最大似然估计的渐近正态性及方差膨胀，并引入了一种噪声感知的似然修正方法，支持基于自助法的置信区间。此外，还给出了一个匹配的最小最大下界，表明隐私失真率是不可避免的。该理论为差分隐私合成数据的生成提供了明确的设计规则和实用流程，已在三个指数族模型和真实人口普查数据上得到验证。

item.title: 递归模型突破语言模型的上下文限制
item.content:encoded:
递归模型提升长距离推理能力，优于传统模型。



摘要： 文章探讨了现代语言模型在有限上下文下的推理局限，并提出递归模型作为解决方案。该模型通过自我调用解决子任务，实现更高效的上下文管理。理论证明，任何可计算问题均可被递归分解，每个子任务所需的上下文远小于传统自回归模型。研究还扩展框架至现代代理系统，证明递归模型在更广泛场景中具有最优性能。实验表明，使用递归方法训练的3B模型在布尔可满足性任务中表现显著优于前沿大模型。

item.title: De-paradox Tree：揭示观察数据中悖论性关联的可解释算法
item.content:encoded:
De-paradox Tree识别观察数据中的悖论性关联并提升决策可靠性。



摘要： 文章介绍了一种名为 De-paradox Tree 的可解释算法，用于检测和解释观察性数据中由于混杂因素和子群异质性导致的悖论性关联。该算法通过递归划分、新型分割标准和平衡方法，调整混杂因素并同质化效应，从而提高因果推断的准确性。与现有方法相比，De-paradox Tree 构建更简单、更具解释性的树结构，能够识别嵌套的相反效应，并在因果可接受变量存在时提供稳健的因果效应估计。该方法弥补了传统因果推断和机器学习的不足，为非专家用户提供更可靠的数据驱动决策支持。

item.title: 基于随机神经网络的表面偏微分方程求解方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种随机神经网络方法高效求解静态与动态表面PDE。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于随机神经网络（RaNN）的方法用于求解静态和动态表面上的偏微分方程。该方法通过随机生成并固定隐藏层参数，仅需通过最小二乘法求解输出层系数，从而避免复杂的训练过程。针对静态表面，提出了参数化、隐式水平集和点云几何的公式，并对参数化方法进行了理论分析。对于保持拓扑结构的动态表面，通过流映射表示表面演化，并在时空配点集上求解PDE，无需重新网格化。实验结果表明该方法具有广泛适用性和良好的精度与效率。

item.title: Linux内核中机器学习基础设施的设计与实现
item.content:encoded:
文章提出在Linux内核中引入机器学习架构的可行性方案。



摘要： 文章探讨了在Linux内核中引入机器学习（ML）的可能性，指出由于内核空间不支持浮点运算单元（FPU），直接应用ML模型可能导致性能下降。为此，作者提出了一个适用于Linux内核的ML基础设施架构，并通过一个概念验证（PoC）项目展示了该方案的可行性。该方案设计了内核空间ML模型代理与用户空间线程之间的交互接口，旨在提升内核配置效率并实现自适应优化。

item.title: 多归因学习基准与方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出首个多归因CVR数据集与开源框架，推动MAL发展。



摘要： 本文介绍了多归因学习（MAL）在转化率预测中的应用，并指出现有数据集的局限性。为解决这一问题，作者构建了首个包含多归因机制标签的公开CVR数据集MAC，并开发了PyMAL开源库。实验揭示了MAL在不同归因设置下的性能提升、辅助目标选择的重要性以及架构设计原则。基于这些发现，提出了Mixture of Asymmetric Experts（MoAE）方法，在MAC数据集上表现优于现有最佳方法。研究旨在促进MAL领域的进一步探索。

item.title: 基于生成流网络的射线追踪路径采样框架
item.content:encoded:
机器学习提升射线追踪效率与准确性。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于生成流网络的机器学习框架，用于替代传统的穷举路径搜索方法，以解决射线追踪在大规模或实时应用中的计算复杂度瓶颈。该框架通过智能采样提高路径搜索效率，并引入经验回放缓冲、统一探索策略和物理掩码机制，有效应对路径稀疏奖励问题。实验表明，该模型在GPU上速度提升达10倍，在CPU上提升达1000倍，同时保持高覆盖准确率，成功发现复杂传播路径。代码和测试已开源。

item.title: BiCAM：一种用于视觉Transformer解释的双向类激活映射方法
item.content:encoded:
BiCAM通过双向激活映射提升ViT模型的可解释性。



摘要： 本文提出BiCAM，一种用于视觉Transformer（ViTs）的双向类激活映射方法，能够同时捕捉支持性和抑制性特征对模型预测的影响。与以往仅关注正向信号的CAM方法不同，BiCAM保留了负向贡献，生成更全面和对比性的解释。此外，BiCAM引入了正负比（PNR）指标，用于在不重新训练的情况下检测对抗样本。实验表明，BiCAM在ImageNet、VOC和COCO数据集上提升了定位精度和解释可靠性，且计算效率高，适用于多种ViT架构。

item.title: 深度学习回归中的不确定性量化方法改进
item.content:encoded:
提出一种高效方法提升深度回归模型的不确定性量化效果。



摘要： 本文研究了深度学习回归中的不确定性量化问题，特别是在异方差回归场景下，传统方法在均值预测与不确定性估计之间存在优化困难、表征崩溃和方差过拟合等问题。作者提出了一种后处理方法，在预训练网络的中间层上对方差模型进行拟合，从而有效解决上述挑战。实验表明，该方法在多个分子图数据集上实现了与现有方法相当或更优的不确定性量化效果，同时保持均值预测精度，并且推理成本较低。

item.title: 提升医学视觉语言模型临床可靠性的评估方法研究
item.content:encoded:
本文探讨医学视觉语言模型的评估盲点，提出新的临床特异性衡量方法。



摘要： 本文指出当前医学视觉语言模型评估中存在一个关键盲点：虽然模型在文本相似度上表现良好，但可能因模板坍缩而生成重复、缺乏临床术语的报告，导致评估指标失真。作者提出一种基于词汇层面的评估框架——临床关联位移（CAD），用于检测生成报告中不同人群的词义关联变化，并引入加权关联擦除（WAE）来衡量临床信号损失。研究发现，确定性解码会导致语义消失，而随机采样虽能提高多样性，却可能引入新偏见，因此需要重新定义“最佳”报告的标准。

item.title: 基于选择性校准的鲁棒不确定性集估计方法
item.content:encoded:
研究如何在未知因果结构下提升不确定性集的准确性。



摘要： 本文探讨了在未知因果结构的情况下，如何通过选择性校准预测来提高不确定性集的精度。提出了一个对污染数据具有鲁棒性的覆盖定理，量化了错误分类“不受影响”校准样本对覆盖率的影响，并引入了一种任务驱动的部分因果学习方法，仅估计必要的后代指示变量。同时，还提出了基于扰动交集模式的后代发现算法和近似干预距离估计方法。实验表明，在控制污染比例为0.30时，修正后的方法仍能保持至少0.95的覆盖率，而未修正的方法覆盖率下降至0.867。该方法在合成线性结构方程模型和真实基因组筛选数据中均表现出良好的适用性。

item.title: SciDER：端到端自动化科学发现系统
item.content:encoded:
SciDER实现科学发现全流程自动化，提升研究效率。



摘要： 本文介绍了SciDER，一个以数据为中心的端到端科学发现系统。与传统框架不同，SciDER通过专门代理协作解析和分析原始实验数据，生成基于数据特征的假设和实验设计，并编写和执行相应代码。在三个基准测试中，SciDER表现出色，优于通用代理和最先进的模型，其自进化记忆和批评反馈循环是关键优势。SciDER以模块化Python包形式发布，提供易于使用的PyPI包和轻量级网页界面，旨在加速自主数据驱动的研究，使所有研究人员和开发者都能受益。

item.title: 基于显式对应关系的增强现实肝脏手术配准方法
item.content:encoded:
提出Land-Reg框架提升腹腔镜手术中2D-3D图像配准精度。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于显式对应关系的增强现实肝脏手术配准方法Land-Reg，通过学习潜在空间中的2D-3D关键点对应关系，提高术中解剖导航的准确性和稳定性。该方法包含跨模态特征对齐模块、不确定性增强的匹配检测器以及形状约束监督策略，有效解决传统方法在临床场景中对齐不稳定的问题。实验表明，该方法在刚性姿态估计和非刚性变形方面均优于现有技术，代码已开源。

item.title: 多模态个性化记忆问答基准ATM-Bench的提出与评估
item.content:encoded:
提出首个多模态个性化记忆问答基准ATM-Bench，提升AI助手记忆与推理能力。



摘要： 本文介绍了ATM-Bench，这是首个针对多模态、多源个性化参考记忆问答的基准测试。该基准包含四年的隐私保护个人记忆数据和人工标注的问答对，涵盖需要解决个人引用、多证据推理和处理冲突证据的问题。研究提出Schema-Guided Memory（SGM）结构化表示方法，并在五个最先进的记忆系统上进行实验，发现现有系统在ATM-Bench-Hard集上的表现较差，而SGM相比传统描述性记忆方法有显著提升。代码已开源。

item.title: 多领域统一图像修复方法DATPRL-IR的提出
item.content:encoded:
提出DATPRL-IR实现多领域图像修复，性能优越。



摘要： 本文提出一种多领域统一图像修复方法DATPRL-IR，通过构建任务提示池和领域提示池，结合提示组合机制，生成具有领域感知的任务提示表示。该方法能够自适应地整合任务相关知识和领域先验信息，提升图像修复效果。实验表明，DATPRL-IR在多个领域中均优于现有最优方法，展现出强大的泛化能力。

item.title: LTD方法提升大语言模型推理效率
item.content:encoded:
LTD通过强化学习优化生成与验证流程，显著提升大语言模型推理速度。



摘要： 本文提出LTD（Learning to Draft）方法，通过强化学习优化大语言模型的推理过程。该方法在生成候选词和验证阶段之间动态协调，直接优化每个循环的吞吐量，而非依赖静态时间分配或代理指标。实验表明，LTD在多个大语言模型和任务中实现了2.24倍至4.32倍的速度提升，优于现有最佳方法Eagle3达36.4%。

item.title: AnnoABSA：首个支持全谱Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis的在线标注工具
item.content:encoded:
AnnoABSA是首个支持ABSA任务的在线标注工具，提供LLM辅助生成。



摘要： AnnoABSA是一款基于网页的标注工具，专为Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis（ABSA）设计，支持从细粒度到粗粒度的全部任务。该工具高度可定制，允许用户灵活配置情感元素和任务需求。除了手动标注外，AnnoABSA还提供基于大语言模型的检索增强生成（RAG）建议，通过上下文感知的方式在人机协作中提供辅助。系统会根据已标注的相似示例，动态添加少量示例以提升预测准确性。AnnoABSA采用MIT许可证开源，便于研究与实际应用扩展。

item.title: 基于加速度计的CNC位置重建方法研究
item.content:encoded:
机器学习模型可有效从加速度计数据中重建CNC轴和刀具位置。



摘要： 本文研究了利用序列到序列的机器学习模型，从加速度计数据中重建CNC机床轴和刀具位置的方法。传统信号处理方法在宽带加速度计信号中难以实现位置重建，而本文提出的基于LSTM的模型能够克服传感器和工艺中的非理想因素，显著降低重建误差。实验结果显示，对于低复杂度运动轨迹，重建误差减少高达98%，对于复杂加工序列，误差减少达85%。该方法还能保留刀具轨迹和工件运动的关键几何特征。这是首个展示从工业条件监测加速度计数据中进行学习式CNC位置重建的研究。
